You are NyayaDraft, an AI assistant that drafts Indian legal documents. You draft documents only — you do not give legal advice, opinions, or predictions about outcomes. You only draft the document types you have been trained to support. If a request asks for legal advice, an opinion on outcomes, or a document type you do not support, politely decline, briefly state what you can draft, and offer the closest supported document type if one exists.

You must always follow these rules:

1. FACTS AND PLACEHOLDERS — Use only the facts the user has provided. NEVER invent a name, address, amount, date, ID number, cheque number, or any other fact. For every detail the document requires but the user did not provide, insert a bracketed placeholder in capitals describing exactly what goes there, e.g. [FULL NAME OF LANDLORD], [REGISTERED OFFICE ADDRESS], [CHEQUE NUMBER], [AMOUNT IN ₹], [DATE OF DISHONOUR MEMO].

2. CITATIONS — Cite an Act, section, or rule only when it is correct and applicable. NEVER invent or approximate a citation. Where a citation is customary but you are not certain of it, write [VERIFY: Act/section] in its place.

3. STRUCTURE — Follow the conventional structure of the requested document type as used in Indian legal practice: title, parties with descriptions, recitals where customary, numbered operative clauses, and the appropriate closing blocks (signatures, witnesses, verification/deponent clause, notarial block) for that document type.

4. REGISTER — Write in formal Indian legal English. Use defined terms consistently ("hereinafter referred to as the 'Licensor'"). No casual phrasing, no markdown formatting symbols inside the document.

5. AMOUNTS AND DATES — Write amounts in Indian notation with words: "₹1,50,000 (Rupees One Lakh Fifty Thousand only)". Write dates in the convention of the document, e.g. "1st day of June, 2026" in deeds and "01.06.2026" in notices.

6. DISCLAIMER — End every document with this exact line as a footer:
"This is an AI-generated draft for review by the parties and is not legal advice."

When you draft a document, output only the document itself, with no commentary before or after it.

Draft a Section 138 cheque-bounce demand notice. Payee/client Ms. Neha Chatterjee, drawer Mr. Nitin Banerjee. Cheque No. 480105 for ₹6,45,000 drawn on Canara Bank, presented and returned (Exceeds Arrangement) per memo dated 01.05.2025. Issued towards refund of an advance.

LEGAL NOTICE UNDER SECTION 138 OF THE NEGOTIABLE INSTRUMENTS ACT, 1881

By Registered Post A.D. / Speed Post

Date: 10.11.2024

To,
Mr. Nitin Banerjee
[COMPLETE ADDRESS OF THE DRAWER]

Sub: Notice of demand for payment of ₹6,45,000 on dishonour of cheque under Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881.

Sir/Madam,

Under instructions from and on behalf of my client, Ms. Neha Chatterjee, residing at Flat No. 39, Sunshine Heights, Viman Nagar, Hyderabad 193216 (hereinafter "my client"), I serve upon you this notice as under:

1. That towards refund of an advance, you issued to my client Cheque No. 480105 dated [DATE OF CHEQUE] for a sum of ₹6,45,000 (Rupees Six Lakh Forty Five Thousand only) drawn on Canara Bank, [BRANCH], in discharge of your legally enforceable liability.

2. That my client, on presentation of the said cheque through his banker, was informed that the said cheque has been returned dishonoured and unpaid vide the cheque return memo dated 01.05.2025 with the remark "Exceeds Arrangement".

3. That the said dishonour of the cheque has occurred on account of your default, and you have thereby committed an offence punishable under Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881.

4. I, therefore, hereby call upon you to pay to my client the said sum of ₹6,45,000 (Rupees Six Lakh Forty Five Thousand only) within fifteen (15) days from the date of receipt of this notice, failing which my client shall be constrained to initiate criminal proceedings against you by filing a criminal complaint under Section 138 read with Section 142 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881, entirely at your risk as to costs and consequences.

5. That this notice is being issued to you within the period of thirty (30) days from the date of receipt by my client of information from the bank regarding the return of the said cheque, in compliance with the proviso to Section 138 of the said Act.

A copy of this notice is retained in my office for record and further necessary action.


____________________
Advocate for Ms. Neha Chatterjee
[ENROLMENT NO. / ADDRESS]

Draft a termination letter on the letterhead of Sunrise Logistics Pvt. Ltd. to Mrs. Trupti Pandey, Branch Manager, with effect from the last working day, on 90 days' notice / salary in lieu of notice. Keep it formal and compliant with Indian practice.

(On the letterhead of Sunrise Logistics Pvt. Ltd.)

Date: 13.08.2026

To,
Mrs. Trupti Pandey
Flat No. 9, Palm Grove, Gandhi Chowk, Mysuru 542099

Subject: Termination of Employment - Branch Manager

Dear Trupti Pandey,

This letter is issued by Sunrise Logistics Pvt. Ltd. (the "Company") with reference to your employment with the Company in the capacity of Branch Manager.

1. TERMINATION — Please be informed that your employment with the Company is hereby terminated, and your last working day shall be 7th day of August, 2026, with effect from which date you shall cease to be an employee of the Company.

2. NOTICE — This termination is in accordance with the terms of your appointment providing for 90 days' notice; the Company shall pay you salary in lieu of notice to the extent of any shortfall in the notice period.

3. FULL AND FINAL SETTLEMENT — Your dues, if any, including salary up to the last working day and other admissible amounts, shall be paid to you in full and final settlement after adjustment of the Company's dues and recovery of its property in your possession.

4. RETURN OF PROPERTY — You are required to return, on or before the last working day, all property, documents, data and assets of the Company in your possession or control.

5. CONTINUING OBLIGATIONS — Your obligations of confidentiality in respect of the Company's proprietary and business information shall survive the cessation of your employment.

We thank you for your services and wish you well in your future endeavours.

Yours faithfully,

For Sunrise Logistics Pvt. Ltd.


____________________
Authorised Signatory
(Human Resources)

By Registered Post A.D. / Speed Post

Date: 10.05.2024

Ms. Meena Mehta

3. That the said dishonour of the cheque has occurred on account of your default, and you have thereby committed an offence punishable under Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881.

4. I, therefore, hereby call upon you to pay to my client the said sum of ₹1,20,000 (Rupees One Lakh Twenty Thousand only) within fifteen (15) days from the date of receipt of this notice, failing which my client shall be constrained to initiate criminal proceedings against you by filing a criminal complaint under Section 138 read with Section 142 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881, entirely at your risk as to costs and consequences.

5. That this notice is being issued to you within the period of thirty (30) days from the date of receipt by my client of information from the bank regarding the return of the said cheque, in compliance with the proviso to Section 138 of the said Act.

Draft a partnership deed under the Indian Partnership Act, 1932 for the firm 'Krishna Enterprises' at Kanpur, business of trading in building materials, between Mrs. Manju Das, Mr. Faisal Sheikh. Capital ₹6,50,000 each, profits/losses in equal shares, with arbitration and dissolution clauses.

DEED OF PARTNERSHIP

This Deed of Partnership is made and executed at Kanpur, Uttar Pradesh, on this 13th day of March, 2026,

BY AND BETWEEN
   (a) Mrs. Manju Das, hereinafter referred to as Partner No. 1;
   (b) Mr. Faisal Sheikh, hereinafter referred to as Partner No. 2;

The above-named persons are hereinafter collectively referred to as the "Partners" and individually as a "Partner".

WHEREAS the Partners have agreed to carry on business in partnership and are desirous of reducing the terms and conditions of their partnership into writing, in accordance with the provisions of the Indian Partnership Act, 1932.

NOW THIS DEED WITNESSETH AS UNDER:

1. NAME OF THE FIRM — The partnership business shall be carried on in the name and style of "Krishna Enterprises" (the "Firm"), or in such other name as the Partners may mutually decide from time to time.

2. PLACE OF BUSINESS — The principal place of business of the Firm shall be at Kanpur, and/or at such other place or places as the Partners may mutually agree upon.

3. NATURE OF BUSINESS — The business of the Firm shall be that of trading in building materials, and/or any other lawful business as may be mutually agreed upon in writing by the Partners.

4. COMMENCEMENT AND DURATION — The partnership shall commence with effect from the date of these presents and shall be a partnership at will.

5. CAPITAL — The capital of the Firm shall be contributed by the Partners in the sum of ₹6,50,000 (Rupees Six Lakh Fifty Thousand only) each, aggregating to ₹13,00,000 (Rupees Thirteen Lakh only), and further capital, if required, shall be brought in by the Partners as may be mutually agreed.

6. PROFITS AND LOSSES — The net profits and losses of the Firm, after providing for all working expenses, shall be divided between and borne by the Partners in equal shares.

7. BANK ACCOUNT — The Firm shall maintain a bank account in the name of the Firm, which shall be operated by such of the Partners as may be authorised by mutual consent.

8. BOOKS OF ACCOUNT — Proper books of account shall be kept at the place of business of the Firm and shall be open to inspection by each Partner; the accounts shall be made up and settled at the close of each financial year.

9. MANAGEMENT — Every Partner shall be just and faithful to the other Partners, shall diligently attend to the business of the Firm, and no Partner shall, without the consent of the others, lend the Firm's money or stand surety for any person on behalf of the Firm.

10. RETIREMENT, DEATH AND INSOLVENCY — On the retirement, death or insolvency of any Partner, the Firm shall not be dissolved but shall be continued by the remaining Partners, who shall pay to the outgoing Partner or his legal heirs the amount standing to his credit, as ascertained from the books of the Firm.

11. DISSOLUTION — In the event of dissolution of the Firm, the assets of the Firm shall be realised and applied, and the surplus, if any, shall be distributed among the Partners in proportion to their respective shares, in accordance with the provisions of the Indian Partnership Act, 1932.

12. ARBITRATION — All disputes and differences arising between the Partners touching the affairs of the Firm or the interpretation of this Deed shall be referred to the arbitration of a sole arbitrator appointed by mutual consent, and the decision of such arbitration shall be final and binding on the Partners.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Partners have set their respective hands to this Deed of Partnership on the day, month and year first hereinabove written.

____________________
(Mrs. Manju Das)

____________________
(Mr. Faisal Sheikh)

WITNESSES:
1. ____________________
2. ____________________

Draft a landlord's legal notice to a tenant. Client/landlord Mr. Rajesh Mehta, tenant Mrs. Sarita Mehta. Premises Office No. 37, Sunshine Heights, Koregaon Park, Hyderabad, monthly rent ₹51,500. 3 months' arrears. Demand arrears and vacant possession within 15 days.

LEGAL NOTICE

By Registered Post A.D. / Speed Post

Date: 09.02.2025

To,
Mrs. Sarita Mehta
[COMPLETE ADDRESS OF THE ADDRESSEE]

Sub: Legal notice in respect of the tenanted premises being Office No. 37, Sunshine Heights, Koregaon Park, Hyderabad.

Sir/Madam,

Under instructions from and on behalf of my client, Mr. Rajesh Mehta, residing at Flat No. 2, Rose Villa, Shivaji Nagar, Chandigarh 391842 (hereinafter "my client"), I serve upon you this notice as under:

1. That my client is the landlord/owner of the premises being Office No. 37, Sunshine Heights, Koregaon Park, Hyderabad (the "said premises"), which were let out to you on a monthly rent of ₹51,500 (Rupees Fifty One Thousand Five Hundred only), exclusive of electricity and other charges.

2. That you, as the tenant of the said premises, have committed default in payment of rent and have failed to pay the rent for 3 months, and a substantial amount has fallen into arrears, despite repeated demands.

3. That your aforesaid conduct amounts to a breach of the terms of your tenancy and has rendered you liable to be evicted from the said premises.

4. I, therefore, hereby call upon you to pay the entire arrears of rent together with the current rent, and to quit, vacate and hand over peaceful and vacant possession of the said premises to my client, within fifteen (15) days from the date of receipt of this notice.

5. That should you fail to comply within the time aforesaid, my client shall be constrained to institute a suit for eviction and recovery of arrears and mesne profits against you, entirely at your risk as to costs and consequences.

A copy of this notice is retained in my office for record and further necessary action.


____________________
Advocate for Mr. Rajesh Mehta
[ENROLMENT NO. / ADDRESS]

Draft a partnership deed under the Indian Partnership Act, 1932 for the firm 'Bharat Trading Co.' at Surat, business of wholesale of textiles, between Ms. Kavita Deshmukh, Mr. Vijay Malhotra. Capital ₹15,00,000 each, profits/losses in equal shares, with arbitration and dissolution clauses.

DEED OF PARTNERSHIP

This Deed of Partnership is made and executed at Surat, Gujarat, on this 3rd day of March, 2025,

BY AND BETWEEN
   (a) Ms. Kavita Deshmukh, hereinafter referred to as Partner No. 1;
   (b) Mr. Vijay Malhotra, hereinafter referred to as Partner No. 2;

The above-named persons are hereinafter collectively referred to as the "Partners" and individually as a "Partner".

WHEREAS the Partners have agreed to carry on business in partnership and are desirous of reducing the terms and conditions of their partnership into writing, in accordance with the provisions of the Indian Partnership Act, 1932.

NOW THIS DEED WITNESSETH AS UNDER:

1. NAME OF THE FIRM — The partnership business shall be carried on in the name and style of "Bharat Trading Co." (the "Firm"), or in such other name as the Partners may mutually decide from time to time.

2. PLACE OF BUSINESS — The principal place of business of the Firm shall be at Surat, and/or at such other place or places as the Partners may mutually agree upon.

3. NATURE OF BUSINESS — The business of the Firm shall be that of wholesale of textiles, and/or any other lawful business as may be mutually agreed upon in writing by the Partners.

4. COMMENCEMENT AND DURATION — The partnership shall commence with effect from the date of these presents and shall be a partnership at will.

5. CAPITAL — The capital of the Firm shall be contributed by the Partners in the sum of ₹15,00,000 (Rupees Fifteen Lakh only) each, aggregating to ₹30,00,000 (Rupees Thirty Lakh only), and further capital, if required, shall be brought in by the Partners as may be mutually agreed.

6. PROFITS AND LOSSES — The net profits and losses of the Firm, after providing for all working expenses, shall be divided between and borne by the Partners in equal shares.

7. BANK ACCOUNT — The Firm shall maintain a bank account in the name of the Firm, which shall be operated by such of the Partners as may be authorised by mutual consent.

8. BOOKS OF ACCOUNT — Proper books of account shall be kept at the place of business of the Firm and shall be open to inspection by each Partner; the accounts shall be made up and settled at the close of each financial year.

9. MANAGEMENT — Every Partner shall be just and faithful to the other Partners, shall diligently attend to the business of the Firm, and no Partner shall, without the consent of the others, lend the Firm's money or stand surety for any person on behalf of the Firm.

10. RETIREMENT, DEATH AND INSOLVENCY — On the retirement, death or insolvency of any Partner, the Firm shall not be dissolved but shall be continued by the remaining Partners, who shall pay to the outgoing Partner or his legal heirs the amount standing to his credit, as ascertained from the books of the Firm.

11. DISSOLUTION — In the event of dissolution of the Firm, the assets of the Firm shall be realised and applied, and the surplus, if any, shall be distributed among the Partners in proportion to their respective shares, in accordance with the provisions of the Indian Partnership Act, 1932.

12. ARBITRATION — All disputes and differences arising between the Partners touching the affairs of the Firm or the interpretation of this Deed shall be referred to the arbitration of a sole arbitrator appointed by mutual consent, and the decision of such arbitration shall be final and binding on the Partners.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Partners have set their respective hands to this Deed of Partnership on the day, month and year first hereinabove written.

____________________
(Ms. Kavita Deshmukh)

____________________
(Mr. Vijay Malhotra)

WITNESSES:
1. ____________________
2. ____________________

Cheque No. 940495 dated 22.01.2026 for ₹2,99,500 was dishonoured for "Funds Insufficient", and the bank's return memo is dated 16.03.2026. Please prepare a detailed Section 138 demand notice through my advocate Harpreet Ahmed. It is being issued from Ahmedabad.

NOTICE UNDER SECTION 138 OF THE NEGOTIABLE INSTRUMENTS ACT, 1881

Date: [DATE OF NOTICE]
Place: Ahmedabad
Mode of dispatch: By Registered Post A.D.

To,
Ms. Ashwini Sharma,
Flat No. 39, Vrindavan Complex, College Road, Aundh, Pune 41133.

Subject: Statutory demand notice under Section 138 read with Section 142 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881, consequent upon dishonour of Cheque No. 940495 for ₹2,99,500.

Madam,

Under instructions from and on behalf of my client, Mr. Arjun Gaikwad, residing at Flat No. 24, Yashwant Smruti, Link Road, Civil Lines, Thane 40037, I, Harpreet Ahmed, Advocate, do hereby address to you this statutory notice:

1. That my client had secured admission with the institute represented by you and had paid the prescribed fees in that regard. The said admission having been withdrawn, you became liable to refund the said fees to my client, the same constituting a legally enforceable debt and liability owed to my client.

2. That towards refund of the said fees, you drew and issued to my client Cheque No. 940495 dated 22.01.2026 for a sum of ₹2,99,500 (Rupees Two Lakh Ninety-Nine Thousand Five Hundred only), drawn on [DRAWEE BANK AND BRANCH].

3. That my client presented the said cheque for encashment on [DATE OF PRESENTATION], and the said cheque was returned dishonoured and unpaid by the drawee bank with the remark "Funds Insufficient", as recorded in the cheque return memo dated 16.03.2026.

4. That the dishonour of the said cheque for want of funds constitutes an offence punishable under Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881. The present notice is issued to you within 30 (thirty) days of the receipt by my client of the said cheque return memo and intimation of dishonour, in compliance with clause (b) of the proviso to Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881.

5. That I, on behalf of my client, hereby call upon you to pay to my client the said sum of ₹2,99,500 (Rupees Two Lakh Ninety-Nine Thousand Five Hundred only), being the amount of the dishonoured cheque, within 15 (fifteen) days from the date of receipt of this notice.

6. That if you fail to pay the said amount within the said period of 15 (fifteen) days, my client shall be constrained to prosecute you criminally under Section 138 read with Section 142 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881, in addition to instituting civil proceedings for recovery of the said sum together with interest and costs, and you shall be wholly liable for the consequences thereof.

7. That a copy of this notice has been retained in my office for record and further necessary action.

Yours faithfully,

____________________
Harpreet Ahmed, Advocate

BETWEEN

Mrs. Trupti Gupta, residing at Flat No. 56, Silver Oak Towers, Aundh, Thane 484520 (hereinafter referred to as the "Licensor", which expression shall, unless repugnant to the context, include his/her heirs, legal representatives and permitted assigns) of the ONE PART;

WHEREAS the Licensor is the absolute owner of and is well and sufficiently entitled to the godown being Flat No. 57, Sapphire Towers, Shivaji Nagar, Aurangabad, more particularly described in the Schedule hereunder (the "said premises"); AND WHEREAS the Licensee has approached the Licensor for permission to use and occupy the said premises on leave and license basis, which the Licensor has agreed to grant on the terms and conditions hereinafter appearing.

AFFIDAVIT

I, Kunal Jain, son of Mr. Suresh, aged about 68 years, occupation engineer, resident of Flat No. 41, Palm Grove, MG Road, Gurugram 581167, do hereby solemnly affirm and declare as under:

1. That I am the deponent herein, and the facts deposed to in this affidavit are within my personal knowledge, and I am competent to swear this affidavit.

2. That the contents of this affidavit are stated by me truthfully and voluntarily, without any pressure or coercion from any quarter.

3. That this affidavit is being sworn by me in connection with the matter relating to one and the same person.

4. That on account of differing spellings adopted by different authorities, my name has been recorded variously in my documents.
5. That the differing names so recorded refer to and denote one and the same person, that is to say, myself, the deponent, and no other person.
6. That there is no discrepancy whatsoever as to my identity and the variation is purely clerical in nature.

VERIFICATION

I, Kunal Jain, the deponent above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 9 of this affidavit are true and correct to the best of my knowledge and belief, that nothing material has been concealed therefrom, and that no part of it is false. Verified at Bengaluru on [DATE].

(Kunal Jain)

Please draft a Memorandum of Understanding between Mr. Manoj Mehta of Chandigarh (First Party) and Mrs. Lakshmi Chatterjee of Gurugram (Second Party) for co-organising a series of skill-development workshops. Keep it largely non-binding except confidentiality and dispute resolution, valid for 24 months.

MEMORANDUM OF UNDERSTANDING

This Memorandum of Understanding (hereinafter referred to as this "MOU") is made and entered into on this 13th day of October, 2024, at Chandigarh,

BY AND BETWEEN

Mr. Manoj Mehta, residing at Flat No. 52, Emerald Park, Wakad, Chandigarh 548572 (hereinafter referred to as the "First Party", which expression shall, unless repugnant to the context, include his/her heirs, executors, administrators and permitted assigns) of the ONE PART;

AND

Mrs. Lakshmi Chatterjee, residing at Flat No. 54, Sunshine Heights, Hadapsar, Gurugram 292775 (hereinafter referred to as the "Second Party", which expression shall, unless repugnant to the context, include his/her heirs, executors, administrators and permitted assigns) of the OTHER PART.

The First Party and the Second Party are hereinafter individually referred to as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties".

WHEREAS the First Party is engaged in and possesses experience relevant to the proposed collaboration; AND WHEREAS the Second Party desires to associate with the First Party for the purpose of co-organising a series of skill-development workshops; AND WHEREAS the Parties are desirous of recording the broad understanding reached between them by way of this MOU.

NOW, THEREFORE, THE PARTIES HEREBY RECORD THEIR UNDERSTANDING AS UNDER:

1. PURPOSE — The Parties shall, in good faith, explore and undertake the training collaboration described above and shall extend to each other reasonable cooperation towards its implementation.

2. SCOPE OF COOPERATION — The Parties shall share such information, resources and expertise as may be mutually agreed in writing from time to time, and shall constitute a joint working committee to oversee the activities contemplated herein.

3. COSTS — Save as otherwise agreed in writing, each Party shall bear its own costs and expenses incurred in connection with this MOU.

4. TERM — This MOU shall come into effect from the date first written above and shall, unless extended or terminated earlier in accordance herewith, remain in force for a period of 24 months.

5. CONFIDENTIALITY — Each Party shall keep confidential all proprietary and non-public information disclosed to it by the other Party and shall not disclose the same to any third party without prior written consent, save as required by law. This obligation of confidentiality shall survive the termination of this MOU.

6. BINDING EFFECT — Save and except the clauses relating to Confidentiality, Dispute Resolution and Governing Law, which the Parties intend to be legally binding, this MOU records the present intention of the Parties only and is not intended to create any legally binding obligation on either Party.

7. TERMINATION — Either Party may terminate this MOU by giving thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the other Party, without assigning any reason, and such termination shall be without prejudice to any accrued rights or obligations of the Parties.

8. DISPUTE RESOLUTION — Any dispute or difference arising out of or in connection with this MOU shall, in the first instance, be resolved amicably through mutual negotiation between the Parties, failing which the same shall be referred to and finally resolved by arbitration by a sole arbitrator to be appointed by mutual consent, the seat of arbitration being Chandigarh.

9. GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION — This MOU shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India, and subject to the arbitration clause aforesaid, the courts at Chandigarh shall have exclusive jurisdiction.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Parties have set their respective hands to this MOU on the day, month and year first hereinabove written.

For the First Party                                   For the Second Party


____________________                                  ____________________
(Mr. Manoj Mehta)                                                (Mrs. Lakshmi Chatterjee)

WITNESSES:
1. ____________________
2. ____________________

LEGAL NOTICE UNDER SECTION 138 OF THE NEGOTIABLE INSTRUMENTS ACT, 1881

By Registered Post A.D. / Speed Post

Date: 26.09.2025

To,
Mr. Faisal Sandhu
[COMPLETE ADDRESS OF THE DRAWER]

Sub: Notice of demand for payment of ₹7,30,000 on dishonour of cheque under Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881.

Sir/Madam,

Under instructions from and on behalf of my client, Mrs. Ayesha Kapoor, residing at Flat No. 64, Riverdale Heights, Gandhi Chowk, Kolkata 193829 (hereinafter "my client"), I serve upon you this notice as under:

1. That towards discharge of an outstanding invoice, you issued to my client Cheque No. 422556 dated [DATE OF CHEQUE] for a sum of ₹7,30,000 (Rupees Seven Lakh Thirty Thousand only) drawn on ICICI Bank, [BRANCH], in discharge of your legally enforceable liability.

2. That my client, on presentation of the said cheque through his banker, was informed that the said cheque has been returned dishonoured and unpaid vide the cheque return memo dated 01.11.2026 with the remark "Account Dormant".

3. That the said dishonour of the cheque has occurred on account of your default, and you have thereby committed an offence punishable under Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881.

4. I, therefore, hereby call upon you to pay to my client the said sum of ₹7,30,000 (Rupees Seven Lakh Thirty Thousand only) within fifteen (15) days from the date of receipt of this notice, failing which my client shall be constrained to initiate criminal proceedings against you by filing a criminal complaint under Section 138 read with Section 142 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881, entirely at your risk as to costs and consequences.

5. That this notice is being issued to you within the period of thirty (30) days from the date of receipt by my client of information from the bank regarding the return of the said cheque, in compliance with the proviso to Section 138 of the said Act.

A copy of this notice is retained in my office for record and further necessary action.


____________________
Advocate for Mrs. Ayesha Kapoor

Under instructions from and on behalf of my client, Mr. Ramesh Yadav, residing at Flat No. 70, Galaxy Heights, MG Road, Bhopal 167093 (hereinafter referred to as "my client"), I address you in reply to your legal notice dated 16.06.2026 (hereinafter referred to as "the said notice"), the contents whereof have been noted with surprise, as under:

PRELIMINARY OBJECTIONS

1. At the outset, my client states that the said notice is wholly misconceived, false and frivolous, bad in law and on facts, and has been issued only to harass and pressurise my client, and is liable to be withdrawn forthwith.

2. The said notice suppresses material facts and proceeds on a distorted version of events, and my client specifically denies each and every allegation contained therein save those that are expressly and specifically admitted herein.

REPLY ON MERITS (PARA-WISE)

3. With reference to the opening paragraphs of the said notice, it is denied that my client is liable to your client in the manner alleged or at all; the relationship and the facts are not as stated.

4. With reference to the paragraphs alleging default/breach, my client categorically denies the same; the obligations, if any, stood duly discharged, and your client has conveniently omitted the correct and complete facts.

I need a detailed statutory cheque bounce notice prepared on behalf of my client Mr. Sachin Kadam, who stays at Flat No. 35, Shanti Niketan, Ring Road, New Friends Colony, Delhi 11009, to be issued through his advocate Abhijit Shinde. My client had given a friendly hand loan to Ms. Pallavi Naik (Flat No. 26, Matoshree Heights, Gandhi Chowk Road, New Friends Colony, Indore 45283), and towards repayment she handed over Cheque No. 136321 dated 03.03.2026 for Rs. 3,67,000 drawn on HDFC Bank, Mumbai Branch. It was deposited and presented on 23.03.2026 but came back dishonoured with the remark "Exceeds Arrangement". Please draft the full Section 138 demand notice. The notice is being issued from Vasai-Virar.

NOTICE UNDER SECTION 138 OF THE NEGOTIABLE INSTRUMENTS ACT, 1881

Date: [DATE OF NOTICE]
Place: Vasai-Virar
Mode of dispatch: By Registered Post A.D.

To,
Ms. Pallavi Naik,
Flat No. 26, Matoshree Heights, Gandhi Chowk Road, New Friends Colony, Indore 45283.

Subject: Statutory notice of demand under Section 138 read with Section 142 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881, consequent upon dishonour of Cheque No. 136321 for ₹3,67,000.

Madam,

Under instructions from and on behalf of my client, Mr. Sachin Kadam, residing at Flat No. 35, Shanti Niketan, Ring Road, New Friends Colony, Delhi 11009, I, Abhijit Shinde, Advocate, address you with this statutory notice, the contents whereof are as follows:

1. That you are well acquainted with my client, and that my client had, out of friendship and goodwill, advanced to you a personal hand loan, which sum you undertook to repay.

2. That towards repayment of the said legally enforceable debt and liability owed by you to my client, you drew and delivered to my client Cheque No. 136321 dated 03.03.2026 for a sum of ₹3,67,000 (Rupees Three Lakh Sixty-Seven Thousand only), drawn upon HDFC Bank, Mumbai Branch.

3. That my client, acting in good faith, deposited the said cheque for encashment, and the same was presented through his banker on 23.03.2026. The said cheque was, however, returned dishonoured and unpaid by the drawee bank with the endorsement "Exceeds Arrangement", as recorded in the cheque return memo dated [DATE OF CHEQUE RETURN MEMO].

MEMORANDUM OF UNDERSTANDING

This Memorandum of Understanding (hereinafter referred to as this "MOU") is made and entered into on this 8th day of February, 2026, at Thane,

BY AND BETWEEN

Mr. Salman Gupta, residing at Flat No. 8, Galaxy Heights, Baner, Thane 470407 (hereinafter referred to as the "First Party", which expression shall, unless repugnant to the context, include his/her heirs, executors, administrators and permitted assigns) of the ONE PART;

AND

Mr. Prakash Khanna, residing at Flat No. 90, Maple Court, Bandra East, Chandigarh 470656 (hereinafter referred to as the "Second Party", which expression shall, unless repugnant to the context, include his/her heirs, executors, administrators and permitted assigns) of the OTHER PART.

The First Party and the Second Party are hereinafter individually referred to as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties".

WHEREAS the First Party is engaged in and possesses experience relevant to the proposed collaboration; AND WHEREAS the Second Party desires to associate with the First Party for the purpose of joint development of an e-learning platform; AND WHEREAS the Parties are desirous of recording the broad understanding reached between them by way of this MOU.

NOW, THEREFORE, THE PARTIES HEREBY RECORD THEIR UNDERSTANDING AS UNDER:

1. PURPOSE — The Parties shall, in good faith, explore and undertake the technology collaboration described above and shall extend to each other reasonable cooperation towards its implementation.

2. SCOPE OF COOPERATION — The Parties shall share such information, resources and expertise as may be mutually agreed in writing from time to time, and shall constitute a joint working committee to oversee the activities contemplated herein.

3. COSTS — Save as otherwise agreed in writing, each Party shall bear its own costs and expenses incurred in connection with this MOU.

4. TERM — This MOU shall come into effect from the date first written above and shall, unless extended or terminated earlier in accordance herewith, remain in force for a period of 24 months.

5. CONFIDENTIALITY — Each Party shall keep confidential all proprietary and non-public information disclosed to it by the other Party and shall not disclose the same to any third party without prior written consent, save as required by law. This obligation of confidentiality shall survive the termination of this MOU.

6. BINDING EFFECT — Save and except the clauses relating to Confidentiality, Dispute Resolution and Governing Law, which the Parties intend to be legally binding, this MOU records the present intention of the Parties only and is not intended to create any legally binding obligation on either Party.

7. TERMINATION — Either Party may terminate this MOU by giving thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the other Party, without assigning any reason, and such termination shall be without prejudice to any accrued rights or obligations of the Parties.

8. DISPUTE RESOLUTION — Any dispute or difference arising out of or in connection with this MOU shall, in the first instance, be resolved amicably through mutual negotiation between the Parties, failing which the same shall be referred to and finally resolved by arbitration by a sole arbitrator to be appointed by mutual consent, the seat of arbitration being Thane.

9. GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION — This MOU shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India, and subject to the arbitration clause aforesaid, the courts at Thane shall have exclusive jurisdiction.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Parties have set their respective hands to this MOU on the day, month and year first hereinabove written.

For the First Party                                   For the Second Party


____________________                                  ____________________
(Mr. Salman Gupta)                                                (Mr. Prakash Khanna)

WITNESSES:
1. ____________________
2. ____________________

MEMORANDUM OF UNDERSTANDING

This Memorandum of Understanding is made and executed at Bengaluru on 08.06.2026.

BETWEEN

Dr. Kiran Bhagat, a medical practitioner residing at [FULL ADDRESS OF FIRST PARTY], hereinafter referred to as the "First Party";

AND

Kale Services LLP, having its office at [FULL ADDRESS OF SECOND PARTY], hereinafter referred to as the "Second Party";

(the First Party and the Second Party are hereinafter collectively referred to as "the Parties" and individually as a "Party").

WHEREAS the First Party is a qualified physician (MD) in practice; AND WHEREAS the Second Party operates a multi-speciality clinic; AND WHEREAS the Parties wish to record their mutual understanding for the First Party to conduct weekly OPD rotations at the Second Party's clinic and to establish referral protocols between them, on the terms set out below.

NOW THEREFORE the Parties record their understanding as under:

1. PURPOSE AND SCOPE: The Parties shall, in good faith, arrange for the First Party to conduct weekly outpatient department (OPD) rotations at the Second Party's clinic and to follow mutually agreed referral protocols. The Parties shall coordinate scheduling, patient handover and clinical documentation as reasonably required.

2. ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: The First Party shall attend the agreed OPD sessions and adhere to applicable medical standards and the clinic's protocols, and the Second Party shall provide consultation facilities, support staff and necessary infrastructure. Remuneration and fee-sharing, if any, shall be agreed and recorded separately in writing.

3. TERM AND TERMINATION: This Memorandum shall remain in force for a term of [TERM OF MOU IN MONTHS], unless terminated earlier. Either Party may terminate this Memorandum by giving 52 (fifty-two) days' prior written notice to the other Party, provided that scheduled patient appointments shall be honoured or duly reassigned.

4. CONFIDENTIALITY: Each Party shall keep confidential all patient records, medical information and commercially sensitive information disclosed to or accessed by it, and shall use and protect the same strictly in accordance with applicable law and medical ethics. This obligation shall survive the termination of this Memorandum.

5. NATURE OF THE MEMORANDUM: This Memorandum records only the present intent of the Parties and is non-binding in nature, save and except the clauses relating to confidentiality, dispute resolution and governing law, which shall be binding upon the Parties. The enforceability of the said binding clauses rests on the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

6. DISPUTE RESOLUTION: Any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Memorandum shall first be resolved amicably through negotiation between the Parties; failing which, the dispute shall be referred to arbitration under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996.

7. GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION: This Memorandum shall be governed by the laws of India, and the courts at [CITY OF EXCLUSIVE JURISDICTION] shall have exclusive jurisdiction in respect of any matter arising herefrom.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Parties have set their hands to this Memorandum of Understanding at Bengaluru on the day and year first written above.

For the First Party
____________________
Dr. Kiran Bhagat

For the Second Party (Kale Services LLP)
____________________
Aarav Wagh, Authorised Signatory

WITNESS:
____________________
[NAME OF WITNESS]

WHEREAS the First Party is engaged in its line of business and possesses experience, resources and capabilities relevant to the proposed collaboration;

AND WHEREAS the Parties, after mutual discussions, have found it expedient to record the broad terms of their proposed collaboration through this Memorandum of Understanding.

NOW THEREFORE THE PARTIES HEREBY RECORD THEIR MUTUAL UNDERSTANDING AS FOLLOWS:

1. PURPOSE AND SCOPE
The Parties hereby record their mutual intention to collaborate, in good faith, towards the objects contemplated under this Memorandum, and to hold such discussions, exchange such information and undertake such preparatory steps as may be necessary to give effect to the said collaboration. The precise terms of any specific arrangement between the Parties shall be reduced to a definitive agreement to be separately negotiated and executed by them.

2. ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
Each Party shall, in good faith, extend to the other such cooperation, personnel, infrastructure and information as may reasonably be required to advance the objects of this Memorandum. The detailed allocation of roles, deliverables and responsibilities in respect of any particular activity shall be mutually agreed between the Parties in writing before such activity is undertaken.

3. COSTS
Save as may be separately agreed between the Parties in writing, each Party shall bear its own costs and expenses incurred in connection with this Memorandum, and no monetary consideration shall pass between the Parties by reason only of this Memorandum.

4. TERM
This Memorandum shall come into effect on the date of its execution and shall, unless extended by the Parties in writing or terminated earlier in accordance with the provisions hereof, remain in force for a period of 12 (twelve) months.

5. TERMINATION
Either Party may terminate this Memorandum by giving the other Party prior written notice of [NOTICE PERIOD IN DAYS] days. 6. CONFIDENTIALITY
Each Party shall keep strictly confidential all information of a confidential, proprietary or commercially sensitive nature disclosed to it by the other Party in connection with this Memorandum, and shall not use or disclose the same to any third party save for the purposes of the collaboration contemplated herein or as may be required by law. This obligation shall survive the termination or expiry of this Memorandum.

7. NATURE OF THIS MEMORANDUM
This Memorandum is a record of the present intention and understanding of the Parties and is not intended to be legally binding, save and except the clauses relating to Confidentiality, Dispute Resolution and Governing Law and Jurisdiction, which shall be binding upon the Parties. The enforceability of the said binding clauses rests on the Indian Contract Act, 1872. Nothing herein shall be construed as creating any partnership, agency or joint venture between the Parties or as obliging either Party to enter into any definitive agreement.

8. DISPUTE RESOLUTION
Any dispute or difference arising between the Parties out of or in relation to this Memorandum shall, in the first instance, be resolved amicably through good-faith negotiation between the Parties. Failing such amicable resolution within a reasonable period, the dispute shall be referred to and finally settled by arbitration under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, by a sole arbitrator mutually appointed by the Parties, and the seat of arbitration shall be at Delhi.

9. GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION
This Memorandum shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India, and the courts at Delhi shall have exclusive jurisdiction over all matters arising out of or in connection with this Memorandum.

10. STAMP DUTY AND REGISTRATION

Draft a partnership deed under the Indian Partnership Act, 1932 for the firm 'Shree Ganesh Enterprises' at Thane, business of trading in building materials, between Mr. Faisal Pinto, Mr. Nikhil Nair, Mrs. Fatima Khanna. Capital ₹21,50,000 each, profits/losses in equal shares amongst themselves, with arbitration and dissolution clauses.

DEED OF PARTNERSHIP

This Deed of Partnership is made and executed at Thane, Maharashtra, on this 18th day of December, 2026,

BY AND BETWEEN
   (a) Mr. Faisal Pinto, hereinafter referred to as Partner No. 1;
   (b) Mr. Nikhil Nair, hereinafter referred to as Partner No. 2;
   (c) Mrs. Fatima Khanna, hereinafter referred to as Partner No. 3;

The above-named persons are hereinafter collectively referred to as the "Partners" and individually as a "Partner".

WHEREAS the Partners have agreed to carry on business in partnership and are desirous of reducing the terms and conditions of their partnership into writing, in accordance with the provisions of the Indian Partnership Act, 1932.

NOW THIS DEED WITNESSETH AS UNDER:

1. NAME OF THE FIRM — The partnership business shall be carried on in the name and style of "Shree Ganesh Enterprises" (the "Firm"), or in such other name as the Partners may mutually decide from time to time.

2. PLACE OF BUSINESS — The principal place of business of the Firm shall be at Thane, and/or at such other place or places as the Partners may mutually agree upon.

3. NATURE OF BUSINESS — The business of the Firm shall be that of trading in building materials, and/or any other lawful business as may be mutually agreed upon in writing by the Partners.

4. COMMENCEMENT AND DURATION — The partnership shall commence with effect from the date of these presents and shall be a partnership at will.

5. CAPITAL — The capital of the Firm shall be contributed by the Partners in the sum of ₹21,50,000 (Rupees Twenty One Lakh Fifty Thousand only) each, aggregating to ₹64,50,000 (Rupees Sixty Four Lakh Fifty Thousand only), and further capital, if required, shall be brought in by the Partners as may be mutually agreed.

6. PROFITS AND LOSSES — The net profits and losses of the Firm, after providing for all working expenses, shall be divided between and borne by the Partners in equal shares amongst themselves.

7. BANK ACCOUNT — The Firm shall maintain a bank account in the name of the Firm, which shall be operated by such of the Partners as may be authorised by mutual consent.

8. BOOKS OF ACCOUNT — Proper books of account shall be kept at the place of business of the Firm and shall be open to inspection by each Partner; the accounts shall be made up and settled at the close of each financial year.

9. MANAGEMENT — Every Partner shall be just and faithful to the other Partners, shall diligently attend to the business of the Firm, and no Partner shall, without the consent of the others, lend the Firm's money or stand surety for any person on behalf of the Firm.

10. RETIREMENT, DEATH AND INSOLVENCY — On the retirement, death or insolvency of any Partner, the Firm shall not be dissolved but shall be continued by the remaining Partners, who shall pay to the outgoing Partner or his legal heirs the amount standing to his credit, as ascertained from the books of the Firm.

11. DISSOLUTION — In the event of dissolution of the Firm, the assets of the Firm shall be realised and applied, and the surplus, if any, shall be distributed among the Partners in proportion to their respective shares, in accordance with the provisions of the Indian Partnership Act, 1932.

12. ARBITRATION — All disputes and differences arising between the Partners touching the affairs of the Firm or the interpretation of this Deed shall be referred to the arbitration of a sole arbitrator appointed by mutual consent, and the decision of such arbitration shall be final and binding on the Partners.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Partners have set their respective hands to this Deed of Partnership on the day, month and year first hereinabove written.

____________________
(Mr. Faisal Pinto)

____________________
(Mr. Nikhil Nair)

____________________
(Mrs. Fatima Khanna)

WITNESSES:
1. ____________________
2. ____________________

Draft a Maharashtra leave and license agreement. Licensor is Mrs. Ayesha Patil, Licensee is Ms. Fatima Tripathi. Premises: Office No. 58, Maple Court, Kothrud, Pune. License fee ₹33,000 per month, interest-free refundable deposit ₹1,30,000, term 22 months, one month notice.

LEAVE AND LICENSE AGREEMENT

This Leave and License Agreement is made and executed at Pune on this 13th day of October, 2024,

BETWEEN

Mrs. Ayesha Patil, residing at Flat No. 6, Galaxy Heights, Wakad, Mumbai 467633 (hereinafter referred to as the "Licensor", which expression shall, unless repugnant to the context, include his/her heirs, legal representatives and permitted assigns) of the ONE PART;

AND

Ms. Fatima Tripathi, an adult, (hereinafter referred to as the "Licensee", which expression shall, unless repugnant to the context, include his/her heirs, legal representatives and permitted assigns) of the OTHER PART.

WHEREAS the Licensor is the absolute owner of and is well and sufficiently entitled to the residential flat being Office No. 58, Maple Court, Kothrud, Pune, more particularly described in the Schedule hereunder (the "said premises"); AND WHEREAS the Licensee has approached the Licensor for permission to use and occupy the said premises on leave and license basis, which the Licensor has agreed to grant on the terms and conditions hereinafter appearing.

NOW THIS AGREEMENT WITNESSETH AS UNDER:

1. GRANT OF LICENSE — The Licensor hereby grants unto the Licensee a leave and license to use and occupy the said premises for the purpose aforesaid, on a purely leave and license basis.

2. TERM — This license is granted for a period of 22 (Twenty Two) months commencing from the date of these presents, and shall stand automatically determined on the expiry of the said term unless renewed by a fresh agreement in writing.

3. LICENSE FEE — The Licensee shall pay to the Licensor a monthly license fee of ₹33,000 (Rupees Thirty Three Thousand only), payable in advance on or before the fifth day of each English calendar month.

4. SECURITY DEPOSIT — The Licensee has, on or before the execution hereof, paid to the Licensor an interest-free refundable security deposit of ₹1,30,000 (Rupees One Lakh Thirty Thousand only), which the Licensor shall refund to the Licensee, free of interest, at the time of handing over vacant and peaceful possession of the said premises, subject to deduction of dues, if any.

5. USE — The Licensee shall use the said premises only for the permitted purpose and shall not carry on therein any unlawful, hazardous or nuisance-causing activity.

6. NO TENANCY — It is expressly agreed and clarified that this Agreement shall not create any tenancy, lease, sub-lease or any other right, title or interest in the said premises in favour of the Licensee, who shall at all times remain a mere licensee, and the juridical possession of the said premises shall always remain with the Licensor.

7. NOTICE / REVOCATION — Either party may revoke or terminate this license by giving to the other one month's prior written notice; on such revocation or on expiry of the term, the Licensee shall hand over vacant and peaceful possession of the said premises to the Licensor.

8. STAMP DUTY AND REGISTRATION — In compliance with the Maharashtra Rent Control Act, 1999, this Agreement shall be registered before the Sub-Registrar of Assurances having jurisdiction, and the stamp duty and registration charges in respect of this Agreement shall be borne and paid by the Licensee.

9. SOCIETY CHARGES AND UTILITIES — The Licensor shall pay the municipal taxes and society maintenance charges, while the Licensee shall bear the charges for electricity and water consumed during the period of license.

SCHEDULE OF THE SAID PREMISES
All that piece and parcel of residential flat bearing Office No. 58, Maple Court, Kothrud, Pune, together with the fixtures and fittings provided therein.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the parties have set their respective hands to this Agreement on the day, month and year first hereinabove written.


____________________                                  ____________________
LICENSOR (Mrs. Ayesha Patil)                                      LICENSEE (Ms. Fatima Tripathi)

WITNESSES:
1. ____________________
2. ____________________

Draft a partnership deed under the Indian Partnership Act, 1932 for the firm 'Eastern Traders' at Delhi, business of running a restaurant and catering service, between Mr. Imran Chauhan, Mr. Rajesh Patel. Capital ₹13,00,000 each, profits/losses in equal shares, with arbitration and dissolution clauses.

DEED OF PARTNERSHIP

This Deed of Partnership is made and executed at Delhi, Delhi, on this 21st day of October, 2024,

BY AND BETWEEN
   (a) Mr. Imran Chauhan, hereinafter referred to as Partner No. 1;
   (b) Mr. Rajesh Patel, hereinafter referred to as Partner No. 2;

The above-named persons are hereinafter collectively referred to as the "Partners" and individually as a "Partner".

WHEREAS the Partners have agreed to carry on business in partnership and are desirous of reducing the terms and conditions of their partnership into writing, in accordance with the provisions of the Indian Partnership Act, 1932.

NOW THIS DEED WITNESSETH AS UNDER:

1. NAME OF THE FIRM — The partnership business shall be carried on in the name and style of "Eastern Traders" (the "Firm"), or in such other name as the Partners may mutually decide from time to time.

2. PLACE OF BUSINESS — The principal place of business of the Firm shall be at Delhi, and/or at such other place or places as the Partners may mutually agree upon.

3. NATURE OF BUSINESS — The business of the Firm shall be that of running a restaurant and catering service, and/or any other lawful business as may be mutually agreed upon in writing by the Partners.

4. COMMENCEMENT AND DURATION — The partnership shall commence with effect from the date of these presents and shall be a partnership at will.

5. CAPITAL — The capital of the Firm shall be contributed by the Partners in the sum of ₹13,00,000 (Rupees Thirteen Lakh only) each, aggregating to ₹26,00,000 (Rupees Twenty Six Lakh only), and further capital, if required, shall be brought in by the Partners as may be mutually agreed.

6. PROFITS AND LOSSES — The net profits and losses of the Firm, after providing for all working expenses, shall be divided between and borne by the Partners in equal shares.

7. BANK ACCOUNT — The Firm shall maintain a bank account in the name of the Firm, which shall be operated by such of the Partners as may be authorised by mutual consent.

8. BOOKS OF ACCOUNT — Proper books of account shall be kept at the place of business of the Firm and shall be open to inspection by each Partner; the accounts shall be made up and settled at the close of each financial year.

9. MANAGEMENT — Every Partner shall be just and faithful to the other Partners, shall diligently attend to the business of the Firm, and no Partner shall, without the consent of the others, lend the Firm's money or stand surety for any person on behalf of the Firm.

10. RETIREMENT, DEATH AND INSOLVENCY — On the retirement, death or insolvency of any Partner, the Firm shall not be dissolved but shall be continued by the remaining Partners, who shall pay to the outgoing Partner or his legal heirs the amount standing to his credit, as ascertained from the books of the Firm.

11. DISSOLUTION — In the event of dissolution of the Firm, the assets of the Firm shall be realised and applied, and the surplus, if any, shall be distributed among the Partners in proportion to their respective shares, in accordance with the provisions of the Indian Partnership Act, 1932.

12. ARBITRATION — All disputes and differences arising between the Partners touching the affairs of the Firm or the interpretation of this Deed shall be referred to the arbitration of a sole arbitrator appointed by mutual consent, and the decision of such arbitration shall be final and binding on the Partners.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Partners have set their respective hands to this Deed of Partnership on the day, month and year first hereinabove written.

____________________
(Mr. Imran Chauhan)

____________________
(Mr. Rajesh Patel)

WITNESSES:
1. ____________________
2. ____________________

LEAVE AND LICENSE AGREEMENT

This Leave and License Agreement is made and executed at Aurangabad on this 23rd day of December, 2026,

BETWEEN

Mr. Manoj Rao, residing at Flat No. 34, Galaxy Heights, Civil Lines, Pune 138556 (hereinafter referred to as the "Licensor", which expression shall, unless repugnant to the context, include his/her heirs, legal representatives and permitted assigns) of the ONE PART;

AND

Mr. Faisal Sandhu, an adult, (hereinafter referred to as the "Licensee", which expression shall, unless repugnant to the context, include his/her heirs, legal representatives and permitted assigns) of the OTHER PART.

WHEREAS the Licensor is the absolute owner of and is well and sufficiently entitled to the shop being Office No. 42, Palm Grove, Bandra East, Aurangabad, more particularly described in the Schedule hereunder (the "said premises"); AND WHEREAS the Licensee has approached the Licensor for permission to use and occupy the said premises on leave and license basis, which the Licensor has agreed to grant on the terms and conditions hereinafter appearing.

NOW THIS AGREEMENT WITNESSETH AS UNDER:

1. GRANT OF LICENSE — The Licensor hereby grants unto the Licensee a leave and license to use and occupy the said premises for the purpose aforesaid, on a purely leave and license basis.

2. TERM — This license is granted for a period of 22 (Twenty Two) months commencing from the date of these presents, and shall stand automatically determined on the expiry of the said term unless renewed by a fresh agreement in writing.

3. LICENSE FEE — The Licensee shall pay to the Licensor a monthly license fee of ₹70,500 (Rupees Seventy Thousand Five Hundred only), payable in advance on or before the fifth day of each English calendar month.

4. SECURITY DEPOSIT — The Licensee has, on or before the execution hereof, paid to the Licensor an interest-free refundable security deposit of ₹2,40,000 (Rupees Two Lakh Forty Thousand only), which the Licensor shall refund to the Licensee, free of interest, at the time of handing over vacant and peaceful possession of the said premises, subject to deduction of dues, if any.

5. USE — The Licensee shall use the said premises only for the permitted purpose and shall not carry on therein any unlawful, hazardous or nuisance-causing activity.

6. NO TENANCY — It is expressly agreed and clarified that this Agreement shall not create any tenancy, lease, sub-lease or any other right, title or interest in the said premises in favour of the Licensee, who shall at all times remain a mere licensee, and the juridical possession of the said premises shall always remain with the Licensor.

7. NOTICE / REVOCATION — Either party may revoke or terminate this license by giving to the other one month's prior written notice; on such revocation or on expiry of the term, the Licensee shall hand over vacant and peaceful possession of the said premises to the Licensor.

8. STAMP DUTY AND REGISTRATION — In compliance with the Maharashtra Rent Control Act, 1999, this Agreement shall be registered before the Sub-Registrar of Assurances having jurisdiction, and the stamp duty and registration charges in respect of this Agreement shall be borne and paid by the Licensee.

9. SOCIETY CHARGES AND UTILITIES — The Licensor shall pay the municipal taxes and society maintenance charges, while the Licensee shall bear the charges for electricity and water consumed during the period of license.

SCHEDULE OF THE SAID PREMISES
All that piece and parcel of shop bearing Office No. 42, Palm Grove, Bandra East, Aurangabad, together with the fixtures and fittings provided therein.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the parties have set their respective hands to this Agreement on the day, month and year first hereinabove written.

Under instructions from and on behalf of my client, Mr. Ramesh Qureshi, residing at Flat No. 60, Sunshine Heights, Civil Lines, Amritsar 168215 (hereinafter "my client"), I serve upon you this notice as under:

2. That my client, on presentation of the said cheque through his banker, was informed that the said cheque has been returned dishonoured and unpaid vide the cheque return memo dated 21.10.2024 with the remark "Account Dormant".

I, therefore, hereby call upon you to pay to my client the said sum of ₹14,30,000 (Rupees Fourteen Lakh Thirty Thousand only) within fifteen (15) days from the date of receipt of this notice, failing which my client shall be constrained to initiate criminal proceedings against you by filing a criminal complaint under Section 138 read with Section 142 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881, entirely at your risk as to costs and consequences.

5. That this notice is being issued to you within the period of thirty (30) days from the date of receipt by my client of information from the bank regarding the return of the said cheque, in compliance with the proviso to Section 138 of the said Act.

Draft a partnership deed under the Indian Partnership Act, 1932 for the firm 'Eastern Traders' at Nagpur, business of wholesale of textiles, between Mr. Ashok Jadhav, Mrs. Rekha Sharma, Ms. Sarita Sethi. Capital ₹22,00,000 each, profits/losses in equal shares amongst themselves, with arbitration and dissolution clauses.

DEED OF PARTNERSHIP

This Deed of Partnership is made and executed at Nagpur, Maharashtra, on this 26th day of December, 2024,

BY AND BETWEEN
   (a) Mr. Ashok Jadhav, hereinafter referred to as Partner No. 1;
   (b) Mrs. Rekha Sharma, hereinafter referred to as Partner No. 2;
   (c) Ms. Sarita Sethi, hereinafter referred to as Partner No. 3;

The above-named persons are hereinafter collectively referred to as the "Partners" and individually as a "Partner".

WHEREAS the Partners have agreed to carry on business in partnership and are desirous of reducing the terms and conditions of their partnership into writing, in accordance with the provisions of the Indian Partnership Act, 1932.

NOW THIS DEED WITNESSETH AS UNDER:

1. NAME OF THE FIRM — The partnership business shall be carried on in the name and style of "Eastern Traders" (the "Firm"), or in such other name as the Partners may mutually decide from time to time.

2. PLACE OF BUSINESS — The principal place of business of the Firm shall be at Nagpur, and/or at such other place or places as the Partners may mutually agree upon.

3. NATURE OF BUSINESS — The business of the Firm shall be that of wholesale of textiles, and/or any other lawful business as may be mutually agreed upon in writing by the Partners.

4. COMMENCEMENT AND DURATION — The partnership shall commence with effect from the date of these presents and shall be a partnership at will.

5. CAPITAL — The capital of the Firm shall be contributed by the Partners in the sum of ₹22,00,000 (Rupees Twenty Two Lakh only) each, aggregating to ₹66,00,000 (Rupees Sixty Six Lakh only), and further capital, if required, shall be brought in by the Partners as may be mutually agreed.

6. PROFITS AND LOSSES — The net profits and losses of the Firm, after providing for all working expenses, shall be divided between and borne by the Partners in equal shares amongst themselves.

7. BANK ACCOUNT — The Firm shall maintain a bank account in the name of the Firm, which shall be operated by such of the Partners as may be authorised by mutual consent.

8. BOOKS OF ACCOUNT — Proper books of account shall be kept at the place of business of the Firm and shall be open to inspection by each Partner; the accounts shall be made up and settled at the close of each financial year.

9. MANAGEMENT — Every Partner shall be just and faithful to the other Partners, shall diligently attend to the business of the Firm, and no Partner shall, without the consent of the others, lend the Firm's money or stand surety for any person on behalf of the Firm.

10. RETIREMENT, DEATH AND INSOLVENCY — On the retirement, death or insolvency of any Partner, the Firm shall not be dissolved but shall be continued by the remaining Partners, who shall pay to the outgoing Partner or his legal heirs the amount standing to his credit, as ascertained from the books of the Firm.

11. DISSOLUTION — In the event of dissolution of the Firm, the assets of the Firm shall be realised and applied, and the surplus, if any, shall be distributed among the Partners in proportion to their respective shares, in accordance with the provisions of the Indian Partnership Act, 1932.

12. ARBITRATION — All disputes and differences arising between the Partners touching the affairs of the Firm or the interpretation of this Deed shall be referred to the arbitration of a sole arbitrator appointed by mutual consent, and the decision of such arbitration shall be final and binding on the Partners.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Partners have set their respective hands to this Deed of Partnership on the day, month and year first hereinabove written.

____________________
(Mr. Ashok Jadhav)

____________________
(Mrs. Rekha Sharma)

____________________
(Ms. Sarita Sethi)

WITNESSES:
1. ____________________
2. ____________________

Please draft a Memorandum of Understanding between Mr. Faisal Yadav of Nagpur (First Party) and Mr. Harish Malhotra of Vadodara (Second Party) for co-organising a series of skill-development workshops. Keep it largely non-binding except confidentiality and dispute resolution, valid for 36 months.

MEMORANDUM OF UNDERSTANDING

This Memorandum of Understanding (hereinafter referred to as this "MOU") is made and entered into on this 7th day of April, 2026, at Nagpur,

BY AND BETWEEN

Mr. Faisal Yadav, residing at Flat No. 64, Shanti Niketan, Baner, Nagpur 303401 (hereinafter referred to as the "First Party", which expression shall, unless repugnant to the context, include his/her heirs, executors, administrators and permitted assigns) of the ONE PART;

AND

Mr. Harish Malhotra, residing at Flat No. 41, Silver Oak Towers, Nehru Nagar, Vadodara 345656 (hereinafter referred to as the "Second Party", which expression shall, unless repugnant to the context, include his/her heirs, executors, administrators and permitted assigns) of the OTHER PART.

The First Party and the Second Party are hereinafter individually referred to as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties".

WHEREAS the First Party is engaged in and possesses experience relevant to the proposed collaboration; AND WHEREAS the Second Party desires to associate with the First Party for the purpose of co-organising a series of skill-development workshops; AND WHEREAS the Parties are desirous of recording the broad understanding reached between them by way of this MOU.

NOW, THEREFORE, THE PARTIES HEREBY RECORD THEIR UNDERSTANDING AS UNDER:

1. PURPOSE — The Parties shall, in good faith, explore and undertake the training collaboration described above and shall extend to each other reasonable cooperation towards its implementation.

2. SCOPE OF COOPERATION — The Parties shall share such information, resources and expertise as may be mutually agreed in writing from time to time, and shall constitute a joint working committee to oversee the activities contemplated herein.

3. COSTS — Save as otherwise agreed in writing, each Party shall bear its own costs and expenses incurred in connection with this MOU.

4. TERM — This MOU shall come into effect from the date first written above and shall, unless extended or terminated earlier in accordance herewith, remain in force for a period of 36 months.

5. CONFIDENTIALITY — Each Party shall keep confidential all proprietary and non-public information disclosed to it by the other Party and shall not disclose the same to any third party without prior written consent, save as required by law. This obligation of confidentiality shall survive the termination of this MOU.

6. BINDING EFFECT — Save and except the clauses relating to Confidentiality, Dispute Resolution and Governing Law, which the Parties intend to be legally binding, this MOU records the present intention of the Parties only and is not intended to create any legally binding obligation on either Party.

7. TERMINATION — Either Party may terminate this MOU by giving thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the other Party, without assigning any reason, and such termination shall be without prejudice to any accrued rights or obligations of the Parties.

8. DISPUTE RESOLUTION — Any dispute or difference arising out of or in connection with this MOU shall, in the first instance, be resolved amicably through mutual negotiation between the Parties, failing which the same shall be referred to and finally resolved by arbitration by a sole arbitrator to be appointed by mutual consent, the seat of arbitration being Nagpur.

9. GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION — This MOU shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India, and subject to the arbitration clause aforesaid, the courts at Nagpur shall have exclusive jurisdiction.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Parties have set their respective hands to this MOU on the day, month and year first hereinabove written.

For the First Party                                   For the Second Party

(Mr. Faisal Yadav)                                                (Mr. Harish Malhotra)

LEGAL NOTICE UNDER SECTION 138 OF THE NEGOTIABLE INSTRUMENTS ACT, 1881

Sub: Notice of demand for payment of ₹2,90,000 on dishonour of cheque under Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881.

Sir/Madam,

Under instructions from and on behalf of my client, Mr. Sandeep Agarwal, residing at Flat No. 48, Galaxy Heights, Deccan Gymkhana, Ahmedabad 114818 (hereinafter "my client"), I serve upon you this notice as under:

1. That towards discharge of an outstanding invoice, you issued to my client Cheque No. 636194 dated [DATE OF CHEQUE] for a sum of ₹2,90,000 (Rupees Two Lakh Ninety Thousand only) drawn on Canara Bank, [BRANCH], in discharge of your legally enforceable liability.

2. That my client, on presentation of the said cheque through his banker, was informed that the said cheque has been returned dishonoured and unpaid vide the cheque return memo dated 24.08.2025 with the remark "Account Dormant".

3. That the said dishonour of the cheque has occurred on account of your default, and you have thereby committed an offence punishable under Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881.

4. I, therefore, hereby call upon you to pay to my client the said sum of ₹2,90,000 (Rupees Two Lakh Ninety Thousand only) within fifteen (15) days from the date of receipt of this notice, failing which my client shall be constrained to initiate criminal proceedings against you by filing a criminal complaint under Section 138 read with Section 142 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881, entirely at your risk as to costs and consequences.

5. That this notice is being issued to you within the period of thirty (30) days from the date of receipt by my client of information from the bank regarding the return of the said cheque, in compliance with the proviso to Section 138 of the said Act.

A copy of this notice is retained in my office for record and further necessary action.

CONSUMER COMPLAINT NO. ________ OF 2025

(Complaint under Section 35 of the Consumer Protection Act, 2019)

Mrs. Nikita Gupta,
residing at Flat No. 45, Sunshine Heights, Kothrud, Nashik 502446

Sunrise Logistics Pvt. Ltd.,

1. That the Complainant is a "consumer" within the meaning of Section 2(7) of the Consumer Protection Act, 2019, having availed of the goods/services of the Opposite Party for valuable consideration, and the present complaint is filed under Section 35 of the said Act.

2. That on 18.12.2026, the Complainant, for consideration of ₹6,65,000 (Rupees Six Lakh Sixty Five Thousand only), availed of the goods/services of the Opposite Party in respect of the matter giving rise to the present complaint.

8. That the Complainant has not filed any other complaint or proceeding in respect of the subject matter of the present complaint before any other forum or authority.

PRAYER

It is, therefore, most respectfully prayed that this Commission may be pleased to:
(a) direct the Opposite Party to refund/pay to the Complainant a sum of ₹6,65,000 (Rupees Six Lakh Sixty Five Thousand only) on account of the deficiency in the services aforesaid;
(b) direct the Opposite Party to pay compensation for mental agony and harassment, together with the costs of the present complaint;
(c) pass such other and further order(s) as this Commission may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

Through Counsel

2. That my client, on presentation of the said cheque through his banker, was informed that the said cheque has been returned dishonoured and unpaid vide the cheque return memo dated 01.12.2026 with the remark "Exceeds Arrangement".

3. That the said dishonour of the cheque has occurred on account of your default, and you have thereby committed an offence punishable under Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881.

4. I, therefore, hereby call upon you to pay to my client the said sum of ₹6,25,000 (Rupees Six Lakh Twenty Five Thousand only) within fifteen (15) days from the date of receipt of this notice, failing which my client shall be constrained to initiate criminal proceedings against you by filing a criminal complaint under Section 138 read with Section 142 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881, entirely at your risk as to costs and consequences.

5. That this notice is being issued to you within the period of thirty (30) days from the date of receipt by my client of information from the bank regarding the return of the said cheque, in compliance with the proviso to Section 138 of the said Act.

Two of us are teachers planning to run a JEE/NEET coaching and educational consultancy near the railway station, and we would like a partnership deed prepared. The firm is to be M/s Bhosale Trading Co. and the partners are Manoj Kaur and Sachin Sheikh. The deed is being executed at Chennai and the principal place of business is Flat No. 20, Om Sai Plaza, College Road, New Friends Colony, Mumbai 40037. Manoj Kaur is contributing ₹4,88,500 and Sachin Sheikh ₹10,68,000 towards capital, and profits and losses are to be shared equally, 50:50. Please draft the deed covering the nature of the coaching business, capital, profit sharing, accounts, retirement, dissolution and arbitration.

DEED OF PARTNERSHIP

This Deed of Partnership is made and executed at Chennai on [DATE OF COMMENCEMENT OF PARTNERSHIP].

BETWEEN
Ms. Manoj Kaur, hereinafter referred to as the "First Partner";

AND
Mr. Sachin Sheikh, hereinafter referred to as the "Second Partner";

(the First Partner and the Second Partner are hereinafter collectively referred to as "the Partners").

WHEREAS the Partners, both being experienced teachers, have agreed to carry on business in partnership, governed by the Indian Partnership Act, 1932, on the terms and conditions hereinafter appearing.

NOW THIS DEED WITNESSETH as under:

1. NAME AND STYLE: The partnership business shall be carried on under the name and style of M/s Bhosale Trading Co. (hereinafter referred to as "the Firm").

2. NATURE OF BUSINESS: The business of the Firm shall be that of coaching and educational services, including the running of a coaching institute for competitive examinations such as JEE and NEET and the provision of educational consultancy, and such other lawful business as the Partners may mutually agree upon in writing from time to time.

3. PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS: The principal place of business of the Firm shall be at Flat No. 20, Om Sai Plaza, College Road, New Friends Colony, Mumbai 40037, and/or at such other place as the Partners may mutually decide.

4. COMMENCEMENT AND DURATION: The partnership shall be deemed to have commenced on [DATE OF COMMENCEMENT OF PARTNERSHIP] and shall continue as a partnership at will until determined in accordance with this Deed.

5. CAPITAL CONTRIBUTION: The capital contribution of the Firm shall be brought in by the Partners as follows: the First Partner, Ms. Manoj Kaur, shall contribute ₹4,88,500 (Rupees Four Lakh Eighty-Eight Thousand Five Hundred only), and the Second Partner, Mr. Sachin Sheikh, shall contribute ₹10,68,000 (Rupees Ten Lakh Sixty-Eight Thousand only). Further capital, if required, shall be brought in by the Partners as mutually agreed.

6. PROFIT AND LOSS SHARING: The net profits and losses of the Firm shall be shared and borne by the Partners in the ratio of 50:50 (fifty to fifty), that is to say, equally between the First Partner and the Second Partner.

7. BANKING: The Firm shall open and maintain its bank account with such bank as the Partners may mutually decide, and the said account shall be operated as the Partners may mutually authorise.

8. BOOKS OF ACCOUNT: Proper books of account shall be maintained at the principal place of business and shall be open to inspection by each Partner. The financial year of the Firm shall be the year ending on the 31st day of March.

9. MANAGEMENT AND DUTIES: The Partners shall be just and faithful to each other, shall devote due time and attention to the academic and administrative work of the Firm, and shall not carry on any competing business to the detriment of the Firm without the consent of the other Partner.

10. RETIREMENT, DEATH AND INSOLVENCY: A Partner may retire from the Firm by giving reasonable prior written notice to the other Partner. The death, retirement or insolvency of a Partner shall not automatically dissolve the Firm if the remaining Partner elects to continue the business, subject to settlement of the outgoing Partner's account.

11. DISSOLUTION: The Firm may be dissolved by mutual agreement of the Partners, and upon dissolution the assets and liabilities of the Firm shall be realised and discharged, and the surplus, if any, distributed among the Partners in their profit-sharing ratio.

12. ARBITRATION: Any dispute or difference arising between the Partners touching the Firm or the construction of this Deed shall be referred to a sole arbitrator, and the arbitration shall be governed by the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996.

13. GOVERNING LAW: This Deed shall be governed by the laws of India, including the Indian Partnership Act, 1932, and matters not specifically provided for herein shall be governed by the provisions of the said Act. The stamp duty payable on this Deed shall be borne as per the applicable stamp law of the State of execution [VERIFY: applicable State Stamp Act schedule entry for instruments of partnership].

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Partners have set their hands to this Deed of Partnership at Chennai on the day and year first hereinabove written.

____________________
First Partner (Ms. Manoj Kaur)

____________________
Second Partner (Mr. Sachin Sheikh)

WITNESSES:
1. [WITNESS 1 NAME, SIGNATURE AND ADDRESS]
2. [WITNESS 2 NAME, SIGNATURE AND ADDRESS]
