Kindly draft a partnership deed for the two of us, both teachers, who are setting up a JEE/NEET coaching and educational consultancy near the railway station. The firm will be styled M/s D'Souza & Bros. and the partners are Rutuja Kadam and Yogesh Pereira. We have agreed that interest on capital should be allowed at 10% per annum. Please include the standard clauses covering the nature of the business, capital, interest on capital, profit and loss sharing, accounts, retirement and death of a partner, dissolution and arbitration.

DEED OF PARTNERSHIP

This Deed of Partnership is made and executed at [CITY] on [DATE OF COMMENCEMENT OF PARTNERSHIP], by and between Ms. Rutuja Kadam, hereinafter referred to as the "First Partner", and Mr. Yogesh Pereira, hereinafter referred to as the "Second Partner", the two of them being hereinafter together called "the Partners".

WHEREAS the Partners are desirous of carrying on business together in partnership, governed by the Indian Partnership Act, 1932, and have agreed to record the terms of their mutual understanding as hereinafter set out.

NOW THIS DEED OF PARTNERSHIP WITNESSETH as follows:

1. CONSTITUTION AND NAME: The Partners shall carry on business in partnership under the name and style of M/s D'Souza & Bros. (hereinafter referred to as "the Firm").

2. NATURE OF BUSINESS: The Firm shall carry on the business of coaching and educational services, including the conduct of coaching classes for engineering and medical entrance examinations and the provision of educational and admissions consultancy, together with such other lawful business as the Partners may mutually agree upon in writing from time to time.

3. PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS: The Firm shall carry on its business from its principal place of business at [PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS ADDRESS], and/or from such other premises as the Partners may mutually decide.

4. COMMENCEMENT AND DURATION: The partnership shall be deemed to have commenced on [DATE OF COMMENCEMENT OF PARTNERSHIP] and shall continue as a partnership at will, until determined in accordance with this Deed or by operation of law.

5. CAPITAL CONTRIBUTION: The capital of the Firm shall be contributed by the Partners as follows: the First Partner, Ms. Rutuja Kadam, shall contribute [CAPITAL CONTRIBUTION OF FIRST PARTNER IN ₹], and the Second Partner, Mr. Yogesh Pereira, shall contribute [CAPITAL CONTRIBUTION OF SECOND PARTNER IN ₹]. Any further capital required for the business shall be brought in by the Partners as mutually agreed.

6. INTEREST ON CAPITAL: Interest at the rate of 10% (ten per cent) per annum, or such other rate as may be permissible under the law for the time being in force, shall be allowed on the capital standing to the credit of each Partner, and shall be accounted for before the divisible profits of the Firm are ascertained.

7. PROFIT AND LOSS SHARING: The net profits and the losses, if any, of the Firm, after providing for interest on capital, shall be divided and borne by the Partners in the ratio of [PROFIT SHARING RATIO BETWEEN THE TWO PARTNERS].

8. BANKING: The Firm shall open and maintain one or more bank accounts with such bank or banks as the Partners may mutually decide, and the said accounts shall be operated as the Partners may from time to time authorise.

9. BOOKS OF ACCOUNT: Proper books of account of all the transactions of the Firm shall be kept and maintained at the principal place of business and shall be open to inspection by each Partner at all reasonable times. The financial year of the Firm shall be the year ending on the 31st day of March.

10. MANAGEMENT AND DUTIES: Each Partner shall be just and faithful to the other in all matters touching the Firm, shall devote due time and attention to its business, and shall not, without the consent of the other Partner, carry on any business competing with or detrimental to the Firm.

11. RETIREMENT, DEATH AND INSOLVENCY: A Partner may retire from the Firm by giving reasonable prior written notice to the other Partner. Road, New Friends Colony, Mumbai 40036, and the deed will be executed at Bengaluru. Milind Deshmukh is contributing ₹10,52,500 by way of capital, profits and losses are to be shared in the ratio 55:45, and we have agreed that interest on capital be allowed at 7% per annum. Please set out detailed clauses on the nature of business, capital, interest on capital, accounts, duties of partners, retirement and death, dissolution and arbitration.

DEED OF PARTNERSHIP

This Deed of Partnership is made and executed at Bengaluru on [DATE OF COMMENCEMENT OF PARTNERSHIP].

BETWEEN
Mr. Milind Deshmukh, hereinafter referred to as the "First Partner" (Party of the First Part);

AND
Ms. Sunil Kaur, hereinafter referred to as the "Second Partner" (Party of the Second Part);

(the First Partner and the Second Partner are hereinafter collectively referred to as "the Partners" and individually as "a Partner").

WHEREAS the Partners, both being engaged in the field of education, have after due deliberation mutually agreed to carry on business together in partnership, the partnership being constituted and governed in accordance with the Indian Partnership Act, 1932, upon the terms, conditions and stipulations hereinafter set out.

NOW THIS DEED OF PARTNERSHIP WITNESSETH and it is hereby mutually agreed by and between the Partners as follows:

1. NAME AND STYLE: The partnership business shall be carried on under the name and style of M/s Sheikh Brothers (hereinafter referred to as "the Firm").

2. NATURE OF BUSINESS: The business of the Firm shall be that of providing coaching and educational services, including the conduct of coaching classes for competitive examinations such as JEE and NEET and the rendering of allied educational consultancy, together with such other lawful business as the Partners may from time to time mutually determine and agree upon in writing.

3. PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS: The principal place of business of the Firm shall be situated at Flat No. 12, Green Valley Society, S.V. Road, New Friends Colony, Mumbai 40036, and the Firm may, with the mutual consent of the Partners, carry on business at such other place or places or branches as may be expedient.

4. COMMENCEMENT AND DURATION: The partnership shall be deemed to have commenced on [DATE OF COMMENCEMENT OF PARTNERSHIP] and shall continue as a partnership at will until determined in accordance with the provisions of this Deed or by operation of law.

5. CAPITAL CONTRIBUTION: The capital contribution of the Firm shall be brought in by the Partners as follows: the First Partner, Mr. Milind Deshmukh, shall contribute ₹10,52,500 (Rupees Ten Lakh Fifty-Two Thousand Five Hundred only), and the Second Partner, Ms. Sunil Kaur, shall contribute [CAPITAL CONTRIBUTION OF SECOND PARTNER IN ₹]. Such further capital as may from time to time be required for the business shall be brought in by the Partners in such manner and proportion as they may mutually agree.

6. INTEREST ON CAPITAL: Interest at the rate of 7% (seven per cent) per annum shall be allowed to each Partner on the balance standing to the credit of his or her capital account, such interest being a charge on the profits of the Firm before division and being credited to the respective capital accounts at the close of each financial year.

7. PROFIT AND LOSS SHARING: The net profits of the Firm, after providing for all expenses, outgoings and charges including interest on capital, and likewise the losses, if any, shall be divided among and borne by the Partners in the ratio of 55:45 (fifty-five to forty-five) between the First Partner and the Second Partner respectively.

8. BANKING: The Firm shall open and maintain such bank account or accounts with such bank or banks as the Partners may mutually decide, and all moneys received on account of the Firm shall be paid into the said account, which shall be operated as the Partners may from time to time authorise.

9. BOOKS OF ACCOUNT: Proper, true and regular books of account of all dealings and transactions of the Firm shall be kept and maintained at the principal place of business and shall be open to inspection and examination by each Partner at all reasonable times. The financial year of the Firm shall be the year ending on the 31st day of March, as at which date the accounts shall be made up and the profit or loss ascertained.

10. DUTIES OF PARTNERS: Each Partner shall be just and faithful to the other in all matters relating to the Firm, shall render true accounts and full information of all things affecting the Firm, shall devote due time and attention to its business, and shall not, without the consent of the other, carry on any business competing with or detrimental to the interests of the Firm.

11. RETIREMENT, DEATH AND INSOLVENCY: A Partner may retire from the Firm by giving reasonable prior notice in writing to the other Partner. The death, retirement or insolvency of a Partner shall not have the effect of automatically dissolving the Firm if the remaining Partner elects to continue the business, in which event the account of the outgoing Partner or his or her legal heirs and representatives shall be ascertained and settled in accordance with the books of the Firm.

12. DISSOLUTION: The Firm may be dissolved at any time by mutual agreement of the Partners, and upon such dissolution the assets and properties of the Firm shall be realised, the liabilities discharged, and the surplus, if any, distributed among the Partners in their respective profit-sharing ratio after adjustment of the capital accounts.

13. ARBITRATION: Any dispute, difference or question whatsoever arising between the Partners touching the affairs of the Firm or the construction or operation of this Deed shall be referred to the arbitration of a sole arbitrator, and the arbitration proceedings shall be governed by the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996.

14. GOVERNING LAW: This Deed shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India, including the Indian Partnership Act, 1932, and all matters not specifically provided for herein shall be governed by the provisions of the said Act. The stamp duty payable on this Deed shall be borne as per the applicable stamp law of the State of execution [VERIFY: applicable State Stamp Act schedule entry for instruments of partnership].

The two of us are teachers planning to run a JEE/NEET coaching and educational consultancy near the railway station in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, and we would like a partnership deed prepared. The firm will be M/s Naidu & Bros. and the partners are Farhan Naidu and Anita Sheikh. Our principal place of business is Flat No. 26, Green Valley Society, Ring Road, Camp Area, Delhi 11063. The partnership is to commence on 16.07.2026. The firm's bank account will be with Bank of Maharashtra, Mumbai Branch. Kindly include the usual clauses on capital, accounts, retirement, dissolution and arbitration.

DEED OF PARTNERSHIP

This Deed of Partnership is made and executed at Bengaluru by and between:

Mr. Farhan Naidu, hereinafter referred to as the "First Partner";

AND

Ms. Anita Sheikh, hereinafter referred to as the "Second Partner";

(the First Partner and the Second Partner being hereinafter together called "the Partners").

WHEREAS the Partners have agreed to carry on business together in partnership, governed by the Indian Partnership Act, 1932, upon the terms and conditions hereinafter recorded.

NOW THIS DEED OF PARTNERSHIP WITNESSETH as follows:

1. CONSTITUTION AND NAME: The Partners shall carry on business in partnership under the name and style of M/s Naidu & Bros. (hereinafter referred to as "the Firm").

2. NATURE OF BUSINESS: The Firm shall engage in the business of coaching and educational services, including the running of a coaching institute for competitive examinations and the provision of educational consultancy, together with such other lawful business as the Partners may mutually decide upon in writing from time to time.

3. PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS: The Firm shall carry on its business from its principal place of business at Flat No. 26, Green Valley Society, Ring Road, Camp Area, Delhi 11063, and/or from such other premises as the Partners may mutually decide.

4. COMMENCEMENT AND DURATION: The partnership shall commence with effect from 16.07.2026 and shall continue as a partnership at will until determined in accordance with this Deed or by operation of law.

5. CAPITAL CONTRIBUTION: The capital of the Firm shall be contributed by the Partners as follows: the First Partner, Mr. Farhan Naidu, shall contribute [CAPITAL CONTRIBUTION OF FIRST PARTNER IN ₹], and the Second Partner, Ms. Anita Sheikh, shall contribute [CAPITAL CONTRIBUTION OF SECOND PARTNER IN ₹]. Any further capital required for the business shall be brought in by the Partners as mutually agreed.

6. PROFIT AND LOSS SHARING: The net profits earned and the losses, if any, sustained by the Firm shall be divided and borne by the Partners in the ratio of [PROFIT SHARING RATIO BETWEEN THE TWO PARTNERS].

7. BANKING: The Firm shall maintain its bank account with Bank of Maharashtra, Mumbai Branch, and the said account shall be operated under such signatures and in such manner as the Partners may from time to time mutually authorise.

8. BOOKS OF ACCOUNT: Proper books of account of all transactions of the Firm shall be maintained at the principal place of business and shall be open to inspection by each Partner at all reasonable times. The financial year of the Firm shall be the year ending on the 31st day of March.

9. MANAGEMENT AND DUTIES: Each Partner shall be just and faithful to the other, shall devote due time and attention to the business of the Firm, and shall not, without the consent of the other Partner, engage in any activity prejudicial to the interests of the Firm.

10. RETIREMENT, DEATH AND INSOLVENCY: A Partner may retire from the Firm by giving reasonable prior written notice to the other Partner. The death, retirement or insolvency of a Partner shall not automatically dissolve the Firm if the remaining Partner elects to continue the business, subject to settlement of the outgoing Partner's account in accordance with the books of the Firm.

11. DISSOLUTION: The Firm may be dissolved by mutual agreement of the Partners, and upon such dissolution the assets and liabilities of the Firm shall be realised and discharged, and the surplus, if any, shall be distributed among the Partners in their respective profit-sharing ratio.

12. ARBITRATION: Any dispute or difference arising between the Partners touching the affairs of the Firm or the construction of this Deed shall be referred to the arbitration of a sole arbitrator, and the arbitration shall be governed by the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996.

13. GOVERNING LAW: This Deed shall be governed by the laws of India, including the Indian Partnership Act, 1932, and matters not specifically provided for herein shall be governed by the provisions of the said Act. The stamp duty payable on this Deed shall be borne as per the applicable stamp law of the State of execution [VERIFY: applicable State Stamp Act schedule entry for instruments of partnership].

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Partners have set their hands to this Deed of Partnership at Bengaluru on the day and year first hereinabove written.

____________________
First Partner (Mr. Farhan Naidu)

____________________
Second Partner (Ms. Anita Sheikh)

The firm will be called M/s Jain Agencies and the partners are Amol Deshmukh and Ajay Apte. The partnership commences on 23.05.2026. Amol Deshmukh is contributing ₹2,44,500 and Ajay Apte ₹11,79,000 as capital, profits are to be shared 60:40, and interest on capital is to be allowed at 10% per annum. Kindly include full clauses on capital, interest on capital, books of account, retirement, dissolution and arbitration.

DEED OF PARTNERSHIP

THIS DEED OF PARTNERSHIP is made and executed at Chandigarh on the 23rd day of May, 2026.

BETWEEN
Mr. Amol Deshmukh, hereinafter referred to as the "First Partner";

AND
Mr. Ajay Apte, hereinafter referred to as the "Second Partner";

(the First Partner and the Second Partner are hereinafter together referred to as "the Partners" and individually as "a Partner").

WHEREAS the Partners, being teachers by profession, are desirous of carrying on in partnership the business of coaching and educational services, and have agreed to record the terms and conditions of their partnership in writing in conformity with the Indian Partnership Act, 1932.

NOW THIS DEED OF PARTNERSHIP WITNESSETH and it is hereby mutually agreed by and between the Partners as follows:

1. NAME AND STYLE: The business of the partnership shall be carried on under the name and style of M/s Jain Agencies (hereinafter referred to as "the Firm").

2. NATURE OF BUSINESS: The Firm shall carry on the business of coaching and educational services, including the running of a coaching institute for engineering and medical entrance examinations and the provision of educational consultancy, together with such other lawful and allied activities as the Partners may mutually agree upon in writing from time to time.

3. PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS: The principal place of business of the Firm shall be at Flat No. 35, Green Valley Society, Gandhi Chowk Road, Shivaji Nagar, Nashik 42215, and/or at such other place or places as the Partners may mutually decide.

4. COMMENCEMENT AND DURATION: The partnership shall be deemed to have commenced on 23.05.2026 and shall continue as a partnership at will, until determined in accordance with the provisions of this Deed or by operation of law.

5. CAPITAL CONTRIBUTION: The capital of the Firm shall be contributed by the Partners as follows: the First Partner, Mr. Amol Deshmukh, shall contribute ₹2,44,500 (Rupees Two Lakh Forty-Four Thousand Five Hundred only), and the Second Partner, Mr. Ajay Apte, shall contribute ₹11,79,000 (Rupees Eleven Lakh Seventy-Nine Thousand only). Any further capital required for the business of the Firm shall be brought in by the Partners in such manner and proportion as they may mutually agree.

6. INTEREST ON CAPITAL: Interest shall be allowed to each Partner on the balance standing to the credit of his capital account at the rate of 10% (ten per cent) per annum, such interest to be computed and credited at the close of each financial year before the divisible profits of the Firm are ascertained.

7. PROFIT AND LOSS SHARING: The net profits and losses of the Firm, after providing for interest on capital as aforesaid, shall be ascertained at the close of each financial year and shall be divided and borne by the Partners in the ratio of 60:40 (sixty to forty) between the First Partner and the Second Partner respectively.

8. BANKING: The Firm shall open and maintain one or more bank accounts with such bank or banks as the Partners may mutually decide, and the said accounts shall be operated under such signatures and in such manner as the Partners may from time to time mutually authorise.

9. BOOKS OF ACCOUNT: Proper and regular books of account of all transactions of the Firm shall be kept and maintained at the principal place of business and shall be open to inspection and verification by each Partner at all reasonable times. The financial year of the Firm shall be the year ending on the 31st day of March, as at which date the accounts of the Firm shall be balanced and the profit or loss determined.

10. MANAGEMENT AND DUTIES: Each Partner shall be just and faithful to the other in all transactions relating to the Firm, shall render true accounts and full information touching the affairs of the Firm, shall devote due time and attention to the business of the Firm, and shall not, without the consent of the other Partner, carry on any business competing with or detrimental to the Firm.

11. RETIREMENT, DEATH AND INSOLVENCY: A Partner may retire from the Firm by giving reasonable prior notice in writing to the other Partner. The death, retirement or insolvency of a Partner shall not automatically dissolve the Firm if the remaining Partner elects to continue the business, in which event the account of the outgoing Partner or his legal heirs shall be ascertained and settled in accordance with the books of the Firm.

12. DISSOLUTION: The Firm may be dissolved by mutual agreement of the Partners, and upon such dissolution the assets and liabilities of the Firm shall be realised and discharged, and the surplus, if any, shall be distributed among the Partners in their respective profit-sharing ratio after meeting all debts and obligations of the Firm.

13. ARBITRATION: Any dispute or difference arising between the Partners touching the affairs of the Firm or the construction or operation of this Deed shall be referred to the arbitration of a sole arbitrator, and the arbitration proceedings shall be governed by the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996.

14. GOVERNING LAW: This Deed shall be governed by the laws of India, including the Indian Partnership Act, 1932, and matters not specifically provided for herein shall be governed by the provisions of the said Act. The stamp duty payable on this Deed shall be borne as per the applicable stamp law of the State of execution [VERIFY: applicable State Stamp Act schedule entry for instruments of partnership].

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Partners have set their respective hands to this Deed of Partnership at Chandigarh on the day and year first hereinabove written.

Draft a Maharashtra leave and license agreement. Licensor is Mr. Harish Patel, Licensee is Mr. Tushar Verma. Premises: Gala No. 58, Sunshine Heights, Shivaji Nagar, Nagpur. License fee ₹57,500 per month, interest-free refundable deposit ₹5,95,000, term 33 months, one month notice.

LEAVE AND LICENSE AGREEMENT

This Leave and License Agreement is made and executed at Nagpur on this 2nd day of March, 2024,

BETWEEN

Mr. Harish Patel, residing at Flat No. 80, Maple Court, Kandivali, Aurangabad 475464 (hereinafter referred to as the "Licensor", which expression shall, unless repugnant to the context, include his/her heirs, legal representatives and permitted assigns) of the ONE PART;

AND

Mr. Tushar Verma, an adult, (hereinafter referred to as the "Licensee", which expression shall, unless repugnant to the context, include his/her heirs, legal representatives and permitted assigns) of the OTHER PART.

WHEREAS the Licensor is the absolute owner of and is well and sufficiently entitled to the shop being Gala No. 58, Sunshine Heights, Shivaji Nagar, Nagpur, more particularly described in the Schedule hereunder (the "said premises"); AND WHEREAS the Licensee has approached the Licensor for permission to use and occupy the said premises on leave and license basis, which the Licensor has agreed to grant on the terms and conditions hereinafter appearing.

NOW THIS AGREEMENT WITNESSETH AS UNDER:

1. GRANT OF LICENSE — The Licensor hereby grants unto the Licensee a leave and license to use and occupy the said premises for the purpose aforesaid, on a purely leave and license basis.

2. TERM — This license is granted for a period of 33 (Thirty Three) months commencing from the date of these presents, and shall stand automatically determined on the expiry of the said term unless renewed by a fresh agreement in writing.

3. LICENSE FEE — The Licensee shall pay to the Licensor a monthly license fee of ₹57,500 (Rupees Fifty Seven Thousand Five Hundred only), payable in advance on or before the fifth day of each English calendar month.

4. SECURITY DEPOSIT — The Licensee has, on or before the execution hereof, paid to the Licensor an interest-free refundable security deposit of ₹5,95,000 (Rupees Five Lakh Ninety Five Thousand only), which the Licensor shall refund to the Licensee, free of interest, at the time of handing over vacant and peaceful possession of the said premises, subject to deduction of dues, if any.

5. USE — The Licensee shall use the said premises only for the permitted purpose and shall not carry on therein any unlawful, hazardous or nuisance-causing activity.

6. NO TENANCY — It is expressly agreed and clarified that this Agreement shall not create any tenancy, lease, sub-lease or any other right, title or interest in the said premises in favour of the Licensee, who shall at all times remain a mere licensee, and the juridical possession of the said premises shall always remain with the Licensor.

7. NOTICE / REVOCATION — Either party may revoke or terminate this license by giving to the other one month's prior written notice; on such revocation or on expiry of the term, the Licensee shall hand over vacant and peaceful possession of the said premises to the Licensor.

8. STAMP DUTY AND REGISTRATION — In compliance with the Maharashtra Rent Control Act, 1999, this Agreement shall be registered before the Sub-Registrar of Assurances having jurisdiction, and the stamp duty and registration charges in respect of this Agreement shall be borne and paid by the Licensee.

9. SOCIETY CHARGES AND UTILITIES — The Licensor shall pay the municipal taxes and society maintenance charges, while the Licensee shall bear the charges for electricity and water consumed during the period of license.

SCHEDULE OF THE SAID PREMISES
All that piece and parcel of shop bearing Gala No. 58, Sunshine Heights, Shivaji Nagar, Nagpur, together with the fixtures and fittings provided therein.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the parties have set their respective hands to this Agreement on the day, month and year first hereinabove written.


____________________                                  ____________________
LICENSOR (Mr. Harish Patel)                                      LICENSEE (Mr. Tushar Verma)

WITNESSES:
1. ____________________
2. ____________________

I would like a detailed legal notice prepared on my behalf. I am Mr. Sandeep Jain, based at Mumbai. On 10.05.2024 I paid a substantial advance of ₹2,72,000 to a Nashik furniture workshop run by Ms. Mary Menon for custom modular furniture for my home. The furniture was never delivered, and despite my follow-ups neither the goods nor a refund of my advance has been provided. Please demand a full refund of the advance with interest at 11% per annum and allow 30 days for compliance, failing which we proceed in court.

LEGAL NOTICE

(Through Advocate / Authorised Representative)

Date: [DATE OF NOTICE]
By Registered Post A.D.

To,
Ms. Mary Menon,
[FULL ADDRESS OF DEBTOR].

Subject: Legal notice for recovery of ₹2,72,000 (Rupees Two Lakh Seventy-Two Thousand only) paid as advance for modular furniture that was never delivered, together with interest thereon.

Madam,

Under instructions from and on behalf of my client, Mr. Sandeep Jain, residing at [FULL ADDRESS OF CLAIMANT] (hereinafter referred to as "my client"), I address you this notice as under:

1. That my client, desirous of obtaining custom modular furniture for his residence, placed an order with your furniture workshop and, on 10.05.2024, paid to you an advance of ₹2,72,000 (Rupees Two Lakh Seventy-Two Thousand only) towards the said work, against your assurance of timely manufacture and delivery.

2. That in terms of the understanding between the parties, the said furniture was to be delivered to my client, and the consideration paid stood liable to be refunded in the event of non-delivery; the obligation to deliver, and consequently to refund, fell due on [DATE PAYMENT FELL DUE].

3. That you have, however, wholly failed to manufacture or deliver the said furniture, and notwithstanding my client's repeated visits, telephonic follow-ups and written reminders, you have neither delivered the goods nor refunded the advance of ₹2,72,000 paid by my client.

4. That your conduct in retaining my client's money without rendering any service whatsoever amounts to a clear default and unjust retention of my client's funds.

5. That I, on behalf of my client, hereby call upon you to refund to my client the said sum of ₹2,72,000 (Rupees Two Lakh Seventy-Two Thousand only), together with interest thereon at the rate of 11% (eleven percent) per annum from the date the amount fell due till the date of actual realisation, within 30 (thirty) days from the date of receipt of this notice.

6. That in the event you fail to comply with this demand within the said period of 30 (thirty) days, my client shall be constrained to institute a civil suit for recovery of the said amount, together with interest and costs, entirely at your risk as to costs and consequences.

7. That this demand is made well within the period of limitation prescribed under law, and nothing stated herein shall be construed as a waiver of any of my client's rights and remedies, all of which are expressly reserved.

This notice is issued to you by Registered Post A.D., and a copy hereof has been retained in my office for record and further necessary action.

Yours faithfully,

____________________
Advocate for Mr. Sandeep Jain

LEAVE AND LICENSE AGREEMENT

This Leave and License Agreement is made and executed at Nashik on this 2nd day of September, 2025,

BETWEEN

Mr. Rohan Mukherjee, residing at Flat No. 67, Shanti Niketan, Kandivali, Nagpur 415816 (hereinafter referred to as the "Licensor", which expression shall, unless repugnant to the context, include his/her heirs, legal representatives and permitted assigns) of the ONE PART;

AND

Mr. Abdul Iyer, an adult, (hereinafter referred to as the "Licensee", which expression shall, unless repugnant to the context, include his/her heirs, legal representatives and permitted assigns) of the OTHER PART.

WHEREAS the Licensor is the absolute owner of and is well and sufficiently entitled to the residential flat being Office No. 10, Galaxy Heights, Wakad, Nashik, more particularly described in the Schedule hereunder (the "said premises"); AND WHEREAS the Licensee has approached the Licensor for permission to use and occupy the said premises on leave and license basis, which the Licensor has agreed to grant on the terms and conditions hereinafter appearing.

NOW THIS AGREEMENT WITNESSETH AS UNDER:

1. GRANT OF LICENSE — The Licensor hereby grants unto the Licensee a leave and license to use and occupy the said premises for the purpose aforesaid, on a purely leave and license basis.

2. TERM — This license is granted for a period of 11 (Eleven) months commencing from the date of these presents, and shall stand automatically determined on the expiry of the said term unless renewed by a fresh agreement in writing.

3. LICENSE FEE — The Licensee shall pay to the Licensor a monthly license fee of ₹12,500 (Rupees Twelve Thousand Five Hundred only), payable in advance on or before the fifth day of each English calendar month.

4. SECURITY DEPOSIT — The Licensee has, on or before the execution hereof, paid to the Licensor an interest-free refundable security deposit of ₹5,90,000 (Rupees Five Lakh Ninety Thousand only), which the Licensor shall refund to the Licensee, free of interest, at the time of handing over vacant and peaceful possession of the said premises, subject to deduction of dues, if any.

5. USE — The Licensee shall use the said premises only for the permitted purpose and shall not carry on therein any unlawful, hazardous or nuisance-causing activity.

6. NO TENANCY — It is expressly agreed and clarified that this Agreement shall not create any tenancy, lease, sub-lease or any other right, title or interest in the said premises in favour of the Licensee, who shall at all times remain a mere licensee, and the juridical possession of the said premises shall always remain with the Licensor.

7. NOTICE / REVOCATION — Either party may revoke or terminate this license by giving to the other one month's prior written notice; on such revocation or on expiry of the term, the Licensee shall hand over vacant and peaceful possession of the said premises to the Licensor.

8. STAMP DUTY AND REGISTRATION — In compliance with the Maharashtra Rent Control Act, 1999, this Agreement shall be registered before the Sub-Registrar of Assurances having jurisdiction, and the stamp duty and registration charges in respect of this Agreement shall be borne and paid by the Licensee.

9. SOCIETY CHARGES AND UTILITIES — The Licensor shall pay the municipal taxes and society maintenance charges, while the Licensee shall bear the charges for electricity and water consumed during the period of license.

SCHEDULE OF THE SAID PREMISES
All that piece and parcel of residential flat bearing Office No. 10, Galaxy Heights, Wakad, Nashik, together with the fixtures and fittings provided therein.

MEMORANDUM OF UNDERSTANDING

This Memorandum of Understanding (hereinafter referred to as this "MOU") is made and entered into on this 23rd day of October, 2024, at Lucknow,

BY AND BETWEEN

Mr. Nikhil Sethi, residing at Flat No. 58, Lake View Residency, Vashi, Lucknow 168281 (hereinafter referred to as the "First Party", which expression shall, unless repugnant to the context, include his/her heirs, executors, administrators and permitted assigns) of the ONE PART;

AND

Mr. Rohan Verma, residing at Flat No. 50, Riverdale Heights, Hadapsar, Gurugram 574076 (hereinafter referred to as the "Second Party", which expression shall, unless repugnant to the context, include his/her heirs, executors, administrators and permitted assigns) of the OTHER PART.

The First Party and the Second Party are hereinafter individually referred to as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties".

WHEREAS the First Party is engaged in and possesses experience relevant to the proposed collaboration; AND WHEREAS the Second Party desires to associate with the First Party for the purpose of supply and distribution of organic food products; AND WHEREAS the Parties are desirous of recording the broad understanding reached between them by way of this MOU.

NOW, THEREFORE, THE PARTIES HEREBY RECORD THEIR UNDERSTANDING AS UNDER:

1. PURPOSE — The Parties shall, in good faith, explore and undertake the distribution arrangement described above and shall extend to each other reasonable cooperation towards its implementation.

2. SCOPE OF COOPERATION — The Parties shall share such information, resources and expertise as may be mutually agreed in writing from time to time, and shall constitute a joint working committee to oversee the activities contemplated herein.

3. COSTS — Save as otherwise agreed in writing, each Party shall bear its own costs and expenses incurred in connection with this MOU.

4. TERM — This MOU shall come into effect from the date first written above and shall, unless extended or terminated earlier in accordance herewith, remain in force for a period of 36 months.

5. CONFIDENTIALITY — Each Party shall keep confidential all proprietary and non-public information disclosed to it by the other Party and shall not disclose the same to any third party without prior written consent, save as required by law. This obligation of confidentiality shall survive the termination of this MOU.

6. BINDING EFFECT — Save and except the clauses relating to Confidentiality, Dispute Resolution and Governing Law, which the Parties intend to be legally binding, this MOU records the present intention of the Parties only and is not intended to create any legally binding obligation on either Party.

7. TERMINATION — Either Party may terminate this MOU by giving thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the other Party, without assigning any reason, and such termination shall be without prejudice to any accrued rights or obligations of the Parties.

8. DISPUTE RESOLUTION — Any dispute or difference arising out of or in connection with this MOU shall, in the first instance, be resolved amicably through mutual negotiation between the Parties, failing which the same shall be referred to and finally resolved by arbitration by a sole arbitrator to be appointed by mutual consent, the seat of arbitration being Lucknow.

9. GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION — This MOU shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India, and subject to the arbitration clause aforesaid, the courts at Lucknow shall have exclusive jurisdiction.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Parties have set their respective hands to this MOU on the day, month and year first hereinabove written.

For the First Party                                   For the Second Party


____________________                                  ____________________
(Mr. Nikhil Sethi)                                                (Mr. Rohan Verma)

WITNESSES:
1. ____________________
2. ____________________

Draft a partnership deed under the Indian Partnership Act, 1932 for the firm 'Krishna Enterprises' at Noida, business of retail of consumer electronics, between Mr. Sachin Pillai, Mr. Rohan Pinto, Mr. Suresh Qureshi. Capital ₹3,00,000 each, profits/losses in equal shares amongst themselves, with arbitration and dissolution clauses.

DEED OF PARTNERSHIP

This Deed of Partnership is made and executed at Noida, Uttar Pradesh, on this 4th day of June, 2024,

BY AND BETWEEN
   (a) Mr. Sachin Pillai, hereinafter referred to as Partner No. 1;
   (b) Mr. Rohan Pinto, hereinafter referred to as Partner No. 2;
   (c) Mr. Suresh Qureshi, hereinafter referred to as Partner No. 3;

The above-named persons are hereinafter collectively referred to as the "Partners" and individually as a "Partner".

WHEREAS the Partners have agreed to carry on business in partnership and are desirous of reducing the terms and conditions of their partnership into writing, in accordance with the provisions of the Indian Partnership Act, 1932.

NOW THIS DEED WITNESSETH AS UNDER:

1. NAME OF THE FIRM — The partnership business shall be carried on in the name and style of "Krishna Enterprises" (the "Firm"), or in such other name as the Partners may mutually decide from time to time.

2. PLACE OF BUSINESS — The principal place of business of the Firm shall be at Noida, and/or at such other place or places as the Partners may mutually agree upon.

3. NATURE OF BUSINESS — The business of the Firm shall be that of retail of consumer electronics, and/or any other lawful business as may be mutually agreed upon in writing by the Partners.

4. COMMENCEMENT AND DURATION — The partnership shall commence with effect from the date of these presents and shall be a partnership at will.

5. CAPITAL — The capital of the Firm shall be contributed by the Partners in the sum of ₹3,00,000 (Rupees Three Lakh only) each, aggregating to ₹9,00,000 (Rupees Nine Lakh only), and further capital, if required, shall be brought in by the Partners as may be mutually agreed.

6. PROFITS AND LOSSES — The net profits and losses of the Firm, after providing for all working expenses, shall be divided between and borne by the Partners in equal shares amongst themselves.

7. BANK ACCOUNT — The Firm shall maintain a bank account in the name of the Firm, which shall be operated by such of the Partners as may be authorised by mutual consent.

8. BOOKS OF ACCOUNT — Proper books of account shall be kept at the place of business of the Firm and shall be open to inspection by each Partner; the accounts shall be made up and settled at the close of each financial year.

9. MANAGEMENT — Every Partner shall be just and faithful to the other Partners, shall diligently attend to the business of the Firm, and no Partner shall, without the consent of the others, lend the Firm's money or stand surety for any person on behalf of the Firm.

10. RETIREMENT, DEATH AND INSOLVENCY — On the retirement, death or insolvency of any Partner, the Firm shall not be dissolved but shall be continued by the remaining Partners, who shall pay to the outgoing Partner or his legal heirs the amount standing to his credit, as ascertained from the books of the Firm.

11. DISSOLUTION — In the event of dissolution of the Firm, the assets of the Firm shall be realised and applied, and the surplus, if any, shall be distributed among the Partners in proportion to their respective shares, in accordance with the provisions of the Indian Partnership Act, 1932.

12. ARBITRATION — All disputes and differences arising between the Partners touching the affairs of the Firm or the interpretation of this Deed shall be referred to the arbitration of a sole arbitrator appointed by mutual consent, and the decision of such arbitration shall be final and binding on the Partners.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the Partners have set their respective hands to this Deed of Partnership on the day, month and year first hereinabove written.

____________________
(Mr. Sachin Pillai)

____________________
(Mr. Rohan Pinto)

____________________
(Mr. Suresh Qureshi)

WITNESSES:
1. ____________________
2. ____________________

BY AND BETWEEN

Ms. Heena Mukherjee, residing at Flat No. 81, Orchid Enclave, JM Road, Visakhapatnam 244778 (hereinafter referred to as the "First Party", which expression shall, unless repugnant to the context, include his/her heirs, executors, administrators and permitted assigns) of the ONE PART;
